item.title: Ground Operations Officer
item.description: The main purpose of the Ground Operations Officer is to help and assist the pilots and act as the link between NHV Denmark and the client. Main Responsibilities Make all the preparation for flights Responsible for planning and coordination for all NHV Denmark’s flight operations Understand the client´s business and needs, under the decision and supervision of the Ground Operations Manager Liaise with customers as required/directed on delays and significant events. Assist with customer enquires or queries as required. Liaise with line internal departments and customers as required on current operational matters. Pilots scheduling Ensure all administration is carried out to the required standard and regulations Anticipate and co-ordinate required changes to the flight schedule and liaise with relevant departments. Assist the colleagues and other ad hoc work from Operation or Ground Operations Manager Responsible for complying with Information Security Management System (ISMS) policies, procedures, and controls and supporting information security objectives

item.title: Finance Manager
item.description: We are looking for a strategic and hands-on Finance Manager to lead our Group Finance function and strengthen our financial foundations for sustainable growth. Reporting directly to the CFO, the Finance Manager provides strategic and operational leadership across Finance, ensuring strong governance, regulatory compliance, commercial insight, and sustainable performance across the NHV Group. As accountable lead for Group accounting and tax compliance, you will partner closely with the CFO and senior leadership to shape international strategy, manage financial risk, and drive organizational performance. You combine strategic vision with operational excellence, continuously enhancing finance processes, systems, and policies to improve efficiency, transparency, and control. Responsibilities and areas Finance Leadership & Team Management Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing Finance team. Build a future-ready finance function aligned with business needs. Foster collaboration, accountability, and continuous professional development. Travel to other bases as and when required. Strategy, Governance & Policy Development Develop and execute the Group finance vision and strategy. Define and standardize Group accounting, tax, and transfer pricing policies. Ensure strong governance, internal controls, and regulatory compliance across all entities. Contribute to strategic planning and executive decision-making. Partner with operational teams to drive performance and growth. Financial Reporting & Consolidation Oversee accurate and timely statutory and management reporting. Lead consolidation across multiple legal entities. Ensure integrity, transparency, and compliance in all reporting processes. Act as primary contact for audits and external reviews. Deliver high-quality financial analysis, insights, scenario planning, and advice on complex accounting and risk matters. Process Improvement & Systems Optimization Drive finance transformation and efficiency initiatives. Optimise ERP systems and reporting tools. Establish scalable processes for a dynamic international environment. Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and safeguard company information and assets. International Expansion & Compliance Lead finance setup for new legal entities in collaboration with external advisors. Ensure compliance with international tax frameworks and transfer pricing regulations. Budget & Operational Management Manage departmental budget and resource allocation. Deliver finance outputs on time and to high quality standards. Travel to operational bases as required.

item.title: AW139 Co-Pilot
item.description: Are you an experienced AW139 Co-Pilot ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential. Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the Den Helder base. Your Role As an AW139 Co-Pilot, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. What You’ll Be Doing Flight Preparation & Execution Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements Assisting the pilot-in-command with administrative flight duties Understanding the client’s business & needs, under the decision and supervision of the Chief Pilot Compliance & Professional Standards Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures Respect company policies including Code of Conduct, Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies Support the pilot-in-command in maintaining a proper standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance Offshore & Team Responsibilities Act responsible to the pilot-in-command in assisting to conduct an efficient flight Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot Remain flexible for operational needs Lead by example and promote team spirit Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs

item.title: Technical Director
item.description: We are seeking an experienced and strategic Technical Director to lead and oversee all technical helicopter operations across our multi-base organization. Reporting directly to the CEO and serving on the Extended Executive Committee, you will hold full accountability for Maintenance (Part-145), CAMO, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, and Part-147 training. You will ensure safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations while driving performance, cost control, and fleet availability. This is a high-impact executive role combining operational leadership with strong business acumen and full P&L responsibility. You will lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, and sustainability programs, positioning the organization for future growth. ⚠️ This function can be performed from any of the NHV bases. Regular travel to other bases is required. Responsibilities and areas Operational & Strategic Leadership Lead and optimize all technical functions (Part-145 Maintenance, CAMO, Engineering, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, Part-147) with a strong business-driven and performance-focused approach. Develop and execute technical strategies, structures, and processes aligned with corporate objectives, ensuring efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and operational excellence. Drive organizational optimization and continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and supply chain operations. Ensure technical capabilities fully support operational delivery, customer commitments, and strategic growth objectives. Establish, monitor, and report on KPIs covering safety, compliance, service quality, cost efficiency, and fleet availability. Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and execute additional executive-level technical assignments as required. Financial & Performance Management Hold full P&L responsibility for technical operations, including maintenance budgets, supply chain expenditures, and lifecycle cost management. Drive sustainable cost optimization and total cost of ownership improvements across the fleet. Partner with Finance to ensure disciplined budgeting, forecasting, financial oversight, and structured performance reporting. Balance profitability objectives with uncompromising safety and regulatory compliance standards. People Leadership & Culture Provide strategic leadership to multi-location technical teams, building strong leadership capability, succession planning, and skills development. Establish clear performance goals, accountability frameworks, and KPI-driven management structures. Foster a culture aligned with company values, embedding safety, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Promote strong cross-functional alignment and transparent executive communication. Safety, Compliance & Governance Ensure full compliance with AOC, (B)CAA, EASA/CAA/ICAO regulations, and internal standards across all technical domains. Oversee governance of Accountable Managers and Postholders (Part-145, CAMO, Supply Chain, Fleet Management, Part-147). Maintain robust operational controls, reporting systems, documentation, approvals, and regulatory records. Champion airworthiness, safety, quality assurance, and consistent adherence to procedures and best practices. Transformation, Innovation & Continuous Improvement Lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, sustainability programs, and SAF adoption. Introduce innovative technologies and structured change management to enhance reliability, safety, availability, and cost efficiency. Drive continuous performance improvement across all technical and logistical functions. Stakeholder Management Build and maintain strong relationships with OEMs, suppliers, MROs, regulators, aviation authorities, and customers. Represent the technical organization at executive and regulatory levels. Ensure external partnerships and contractual commitments align with internal technical strategy and support business growth.

item.title: Flight Operations Officer
item.description: The Flight Operations Officer is responsible for the execution of the daily base flight schedule for our base in Den Helder. They will interface with a number of internal and external stakeholders to manage the safe, compliant and efficient delivery of the program, whilst upholding a high standard of service to NHV’s clients. The position is an operational as well as administrative function, part of the operations department, and reports directly to the Service Delivery Lead. This is a part-time function (20 hours / week). Depending on the candidate and preferences, this could be extended. Responsibilities and areas Flight Planning & Coordination Prepare and maintain daily flight schedules, including flight watch and monitoring. Anticipate and coordinate required changes to the flight schedule. Provide operational paperwork and briefings to flight crews. Monitor weather and NOTAMs relevant to flight operations. Crew Management Manage pilot scheduling and ensure compliance with Flight Time Limitations (FTL). Liaise with crew members regarding operational updates and changes. Client & Stakeholder Liaison Act as a key point of contact for clients regarding flight changes, disruptions, and queries. Liaise with Air Traffic Control and airport authorities as needed. Communicate with internal departments (Engineering, Ground Operations, etc.) to ensure fleet availability and operational readiness. Administration & Compliance Ensure all operational and customer reports are completed accurately and on time. Maintain high standards of documentation in line with regulatory and company requirements.

item.title: AW139 Captain
item.description: Are you an experienced AW139 Captain ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential. Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the Den Helder base. Your Role As an AW139 Captain, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. You report to the Chief Pilot and play a key role in representing the company towards our offshore clients. What You’ll Be Doing Flight Preparation & Execution Perform full technical preparation of flights (route planning, fuel calculations, aircraft inspection) Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements Ensure all aircraft safety systems are fully operational Complete accurate pre- and post-flight administration Report any incidents, defects or irregularities Compliance & Professional Standards Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures Respect company policies including Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies Maintain high standards of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance Offshore & Team Responsibilities Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot Remain flexible for operational needs Demonstrate strong customer service awareness Lead by example and promote team spirit Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs

item.title: Experienced HHO Operator – Licensed Engineer
item.description: The helicopter Hoist Operator plays a critical role combining technical skills as a Licensed Line Engineer working on the helicopter and performing hoist flights hoisting passengers to the wind farms. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests. Responsibilities and Main Results Areas Helicopter Hoist operator (HHO) Carry out HHO flight duties, in accordance with NHV Operations SOP's. Know the NHV procedures and the Flight Operations Manual. Inspect hoist systems, cables, hooks, harnesses, and safety equipment. Conduct safety briefings with passengers and flight crew. Coordinate passenger- and aircraft handling procedures before and after flights. Assist with the technical preparation for the flight(s). Operate hoist controls smoothly to raise/lower personnel or cargo. Provide precise directional guidance to the pilot (clearance, obstacles, positioning). Immediately halt operations if unsafe conditions arise. Use standardized aviation terminology. Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as respecting the Drugs & Alcohol policy. Line Maintenance Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable. Ensure all safety regulations are followed Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system. Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures. Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous. Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified. Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings. Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks. Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order. Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity. Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs. Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving.

item.title: B1.3 Engineer AW139
item.description: Are you an experienced EASA Part 66 B1.3 Licensed Engineer ready to work in a dynamic offshore helicopter environment? At our Den Helder base, you will play a key role in keeping our AW139 fleet mission-ready and safe. You’ll join a close-knit team of 8 engineers, working in a professional yet supportive environment where safety, teamwork and technical excellence come first. Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the base. Your Role As a B1.3 Engineer, you are responsible for performing and certifying line maintenance in accordance with approved maintenance data and company procedures. You work closely with fellow engineers under the supervision of the Shift Supervisor, reporting hierarchically to the Base Manager and functionally to the Maintenance Lead. You are more than a certifying engineer — you are a problem solver, safety ambassador and key contributor to operational continuity. What You’ll Be Doing Line Maintenance & Technical Excellence Perform and certify maintenance tasks in accordance with approved maintenance data Execute work orders while respecting all applicable restrictions Raise non-routine tasks and defect entries for unscheduled findings Ensure Independent Inspections on safety-critical tasks Record and certify all maintenance accurately in the work file Safety & Compliance Follow all safety regulations and MOE procedures Report safety hazards and occurrences via the reporting system Flag inaccurate, incomplete or unclear procedures Maintain a clean and orderly workspace with properly maintained tools Operational Support Contribute to flexible and efficient work planning Ensure maintenance is completed within operational deadlines Coordinate with Supply Chain, Stores and support services to guarantee parts availability Perform daily technical analysis and problem-solving based on crew feedback

item.title: B1.3 and B2 H175 Engineer
item.description: The helicopter B1.3 or B2 Engineer plays a critical role ensuring the helicopter is ready and fit for flight and is in an airworthy condition. Performing all required scheduled and unscheduled line maintenance. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations for crew changes and hoisting on wind farms. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests. Responsibilities and Main Results Areas Line Maintenance - Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions - Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable. - Ensure all safety regulations are followed - Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system. - Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures. - Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous. - Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified. - Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings. - Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks. - Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order. - Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity. - Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs. - Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. - Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving. Hoist Operations - A combination of an Engineering position and a Helicopter Hoist Operator (HHO) position is a possibility but not a requirement. Refer to the job description HHO for more details.

item.title: H175 Co-Pilot
item.description: To execute scheduled offshore energy flights in the safest and most efficient manner in accordance with aviation regulation and whilst upholding NHV standards. Responsibilities Assisting the Pilot-in-Command with the technical preparation of flights including, but not limited to, route planning, fuel calculation, performance limitations and general inspection of the helicopter Ensuring that all pre and post flight administration is completed accurately under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command Assisting the Pilot-in-Command as required with the raising of any reportable incidents and issues post flight via the correct channel. Conducting pilot duties under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command whilst fully complying with all safety rules, requirements, limitations and SOPs with full regard to the helicopter type and scope of flight operation Working alongside the Pilot-in-Command to ensure that all safety systems and equipment are functioning correctly Maintaining a good working knowledge of NHV Ops Manuals, Notices To Pilots, other related manuals, instructions and procedures Consistently demonstrating a good awareness of customer service requirements and understanding the client's business & needs, under the supervision of the Chief Pilot. Performing other assigned flying duties as required Maintaining familiarity with all relevant national and international air legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures. Maintaining the correct standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance. Completing tasks allocated by the Chief Pilot as required Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as adhering to the Drugs & Alcohol policy.

item.title: Mechanic - Aberdeen
item.description: NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. We aim to create a leading position in the helicopter world with a primary focus on Europe and Africa, without compromising our safety. We strive to provide an increasing level of flexibility to our customers. Our services and experience present a refreshing and safe alternative in the industry. NHV cares about it’s people and invests in them to shape the best professionals in the industry. At NHV, we are dedicated to delivering excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions. Responsibilities Line Maintenance - Basic tasks in Line Maintenance, in compliance with the requirements as described in the regulations: refueling, towing, baggage loading and other line activities; - Ensure that all these line maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the UK CAA and M.O.E. instructions; - Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to ensure provision of a serviceable aircraft to meet the operational need; - Assist the licensed engineers in trouble shooting; - Assist the licensed engineers in correct handling of maintenance tasks – final check to be done by the licensed engineers. - Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain, Store that the necessary maintenance material and spare parts are present on site at the planned moment: material in service car, consumables, limited service stock, work material, material needed for normal day-to-day line maintenance, exceptional material supplied for exceptional line maintenance or small repair tasks; - Report daily to the licensed engineer on completion of tasks. Base Maintenance - Comply with the requirements as described in the regulations; - Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the UK CAA and M.O.E. instructions; - Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to ensure provision of serviceable aircraft to meet the operational need; - Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain, Store & MCC that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment.

item.title: B2 Engineer
item.description: About NHV NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. We aim to create a leading position in the helicopter world with a primary focus on Europe and Africa, without compromising our safety. We strive to provide an increasing level of flexibility to our customers. Our services and experience present a refreshing and safe alternative in the industry. NHV cares about it’s people and invests in them to shape the best professionals in the industry. At NHV, we are dedicated to delivering excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions. Responsibilities - Comply with the requirements as described in the regulations; - Ensure that all line maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the UK CAA and M.O.E. instructions; - Ensure, together with all Engineers, a variable and flexible work planning and work roster based on business needs that supports and realizes business continuity; - Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to ensure provision of a serviceable aircraft to meet the operational need; - Ensure, based on pilot and all other relevant information, daily problem analysis and problem solving; - Ensure briefing and debriefing of pilots and Maintenance Lead; - Ensure that PRF (preflight check) is performed; - Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain, Store and support services that the necessary maintenance material and spare parts are present on site at the planned moment: material in service car, consumables, limited service stock, work material, material needed for normal day-to-day line maintenance, exceptional material supplied for exceptional line maintenance or small repair tasks; - Report daily on the maintenance situation to the Maintenance Lead.

item.title: Technical Safety and Compliance Manager
item.description: The role will involve travel to NHV bases and supplier sites will be required. The Technical Safety and Compliance Manager co-operates closely with all organisational subdivisions of NHV. Responsibilities and areas - Develop and execute an internal audit plan and lead and manage internal audits, including scoping, planning. - Collaborate with cross functional teams to ensure timely completion of audit projects and prepare and present audit findings and recommendations. - Develop and improve audit checklists and audit reports. - Execute product and process audits to check the conformity of the work steps according to the technical product and process requirements, to verify compliance of operational processes with aviation regulations, industry standards, corporate guidelines, or operational procedures, either internally or externally. - Coach and train auditee to understand the requirements. - Documentation of audit results in audit reports, definition of deviations, and discussion of the results with the relevant departments or suppliers. - Documentation of deviations, monitoring deadlines, and tracking corrective and preventive actions. - Support of the departments in the definition, initiation, and implementation of corrective and preventive actions. - Daily management of safety cases in the reporting system

item.title: Helicopter mechanic - Lanvéoc
item.description: Responsibilities & areas Maintenance tasks on aircraft and components in accordance with issued work orders, applicable manuals, and approved maintenance data. Carry out activities such as: Disassembly & reassembly Rectify defects Component replacements and system adjustments Ensure all safety regulations and procedures are followed at all times; Immediately report safety hazards or mandatory occurrences via the NHV occurrence reporting system; Follow all MOE procedures and notify the Chief Engineer/Maintenance Lead if instructions or processes are unclear, incomplete, or ambiguous; Record all performed maintenance accurately and clearly in the work file; Raise defect entries or non-routine tasks for any findings discovered during inspections; Support Licensed Engineers during troubleshooting and functional checks; Maintain a clean, safe, and organized working environment, including proper care of tools and ground equipment; Contribute to a flexible and efficient planning; Help ensure that planned maintenance is completed within appropriate timeframes to support operational needs.

item.title: B2 Engineer - Lanvéoc
item.description: Responsibilities & areas Perform base & line maintenance on aircraft and components as per work orders; Perform effective troubleshooting based on pilot reports and system indications; Ensure that all maintenance tasks are carried out, following all approved maintenance data, procedures (MOE), and airworthiness regulations; Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to have a serviceable aircraft that meets the operational need; Accurately certify completed tasks and ensure proper documentation in the work pack; Ensure proper handling, segregation, and documentation of removed/installed components; Adhere to safety standards; report hazards and occurrences via the NHV reporting system; Ensure briefing & debriefing of the Chief Engineer and/or Maintenance Lead; Report daily on the maintenance situation; Inform and update the CE/ML on the maintenance situation; Certify B2 category maintenance activities in accordance with the regulations; Work independently when performing in a less controlled environment and take initiative to improve work, show flexibility within the team to complete tasks within appropriate time scales.

item.title: B1.3 Engineer - Lanvéoc
item.description: Responsibilities & areas Perform base & line maintenance on aircraft and components as per work orders; Perform effective troubleshooting based on pilot reports and system indications; Ensure that all maintenance tasks are carried out, following all approved maintenance data, procedures (MOE), and airworthiness regulations; Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to have a serviceable aircraft that meets the operational need; Accurately certify completed tasks and ensure proper documentation in the work pack; Ensure proper handling, segregation, and documentation of removed/installed components; Adhere to safety standards; report hazards and occurrences via the NHV reporting system; Ensure briefing & debriefing of the Chief Engineer and/or Maintenance Lead; Report daily on the maintenance situation; Inform and update the CE/ML on the maintenance situation; Certify B1.3 category maintenance activities in accordance with the regulations; Work independently when performing in a less controlled environment and take initiative to improve work, show flexibility within the team to complete tasks within appropriate time scales.

item.title: Mechanic
item.description: As a Mechanic in the base maintenance hangar of Ostend, you will be responsible for performing heavy base maintenance on Airbus H175 and H145 helicopters. You will work as part of the base maintenance team under coordination of the Dock Chief and in collaboration with Licensed Engineers. With the right experience and progression, this role offers the opportunity to evolve into a Licensed Engineer position. Responsibilities & Main Result Areas Maintenance tasks on aircraft and components in accordance with issued work orders, applicable manuals, and approved maintenance data. Carry out activities such as: Disassembly & reassembly Major inspections (H175 heavy checks, H145 scheduled inspections) Rectify defects Component replacements and system adjustments Ensure all safety regulations, PPE requirements, and hangar procedures are followed at all times. Immediately report safety hazards or mandatory occurrences via the NHV occurrence reporting system. Follow all MOE procedures and notify the Dock Chief if instructions or processes are unclear, incomplete, or ambiguous. Record all performed maintenance accurately and clearly in the work file. Raise defect entries or non-routine tasks for any findings discovered during inspections. Support Licensed Engineers during troubleshooting and functional checks. Maintain a clean, safe, and organized working environment, including proper care of tools and ground equipment. Contribute to a flexible and efficient planning of workload and support roster based on business continuity. Help ensure that planned maintenance is completed within appropriate timeframes to support operational needs.

item.title: Co-Pilot
item.description: Responsibilities and Main Result Areas - Assisting the Pilot-in-Command with the technical preparation of flights including, but not limited to, route planning, fuel calculation, performance limitations and general inspection of the helicopter - Ensuring that all pre and post flight administration is completed accurately under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command - Assisting the Pilot-in-Command as required with the raising of any reportable incidents and issues post flight via the correct channel. - Conducting pilot duties under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command whilst fully complying with all safety rules, requirements, limitations and SOPs with full regard to the helicopter type and scope of flight operation - Working alongside the Pilot-in-Command to ensure that all safety systems and equipment are functioning correctly - Maintaining a good working knowledge of NHV Operations Manuals, Notices to Pilots, other related manuals, instructions and procedures - Consistently demonstrating a good awareness of customer service requirements and understanding the client's needs, under the supervision of the Chief Pilot. - Performing other assigned flying duties as required - Maintaining familiarity with all relevant national and international air legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures. - Maintaining the correct standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance. - Completing tasks allocated by the Flight Operations Department as required - Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. - Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as adhering to the Drugs and Alcohol policy.

item.title: Executive Assistant
item.description: The Executive Assistant (EA) provides high-level administrative and strategic support to the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and Executive Committee (ExCom). This role requires exceptional organizational skills, discretion, professionalism, and the ability to manage complex priorities in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. The EA acts as a trusted partner, ensuring seamless coordination, effective communication, with a proactive approach to day to day matters within the Executive Office. Responsibilities and Main Result Areas Executive Support to the CEO Manage the CEO’s daily calendar, appointments, travel arrangements, and correspondence. Attend meetings with the CEO as necessary, prepare agenda’s, taking of minutes and preparing presentations / speeches, where required. Following up on actions from meetings where required. Ensure the CEO is fully prepared for all meetings, events, and external engagements. Act as a gatekeeper and liaison between the CEO and internal/external stakeholders. Handle confidential information with the highest level of integrity and discretion. Support to Executive Committee (ExCom) Facilitate and manage recurrent reporting to the Board of Directors. Prepare and act as secretary for the Board of Directors. Facilitate communication and alignment among ExCom members and departments. Support in the planning of leadership offsites, strategy sessions, and special projects. Assist in preparing meetings (agenda, content development, practical arrangements, catering, set up). Coordinate the booking of all meeting rooms at HQ for all NHV users and give support accordingly for changes, double bookings, requests for extra availability. Manage business travel itineraries for the ExCom team members. General Liaise with company departments and bases as required to facilitate senior management coordination. Travel to other Bases/Countries when required.

item.title: B2 engineer, helicopter engineer, aviation engineer, H175, H145, Airbus Helicopters, Airbus, Leonardo, aviation, engineering, maintenance, aviation technique
item.description: Are you an experienced EASA Part 66 B2 Licensed Engineer ready to work in a dynamic offshore helicopter environment? At our Ostend base, you will play a key role in keeping our Airbus fleet mission-ready and safe. You’ll join a close-knit team of 35 engineers, working in a professional yet supportive environment where safety, teamwork and technical excellence come first. Your Role As a B2 Engineer, you are responsible for performing and certifying line maintenance in accordance with approved maintenance data and company procedures. You work closely with fellow engineers and report to the Chief Engineer. You are more than a certifying engineer — you are a problem solver, safety ambassador and key contributor to operational continuity. What You’ll Be Doing Line Maintenance & Technical Excellence Perform base maintenance and modifications on aircraft and components as per work orders Certify B2 category maintenance activities in accordance with the regulations On request and ad hoc, also performing line maintenance duties Execute work orders while respecting all applicable restrictions Raise non-routine tasks and defect entries for unscheduled findings Ensure Independent Inspections on safety-critical tasks Record and certify all maintenance accurately in the work file Properly label and separate all removed components and ensure all installed components have the proper airworthiness documents available Participate in the daily morning Chief Engineer's briefing Ensure continuous briefing & debriefing of the Chief Engineer on the maintenance situation Safety & Compliance Follow all safety regulations and BCAA/EASA & MOE procedures Apply all tool-, POL and consumable control procedures Report safety hazards and occurrences via the reporting system Flag inaccurate, incomplete or unclear procedures Maintain a clean and orderly workspace with properly maintained tools Operational Support Contribute to flexible and efficient work planning Ensure maintenance is completed within operational deadlines Coordinate with Supply Chain and Stores to guarantee parts availability Perform effective electrical and avionic troubleshooting based on crew feedback and system indications

item.title: B1.3 Engineer
item.description: The helicopter B1.3 Engineer is responsible for the maintenance (line/base) of the helicopters. He/she works in a team of engineers and reports to the Chief Engineer. Responsibilities & areas Line Maintenance Comply with the requirements as described in the regulations; Ensure that all line maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the UK CAA and M.O.E. instructions; Ensure, together with all Engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business needs that supports & realizes business continuity; Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to ensure provision of a serviceable aircraft to meet the operational need; Ensure, based on pilot & all other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving; Ensure briefing & debriefing of pilots and Chief Engineer; Ensure that PRF (preflight check) is performed; Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain, Store & support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment: material in service car, consumables, limited service stock, work material, material needed for normal day-to-day line maintenance, exceptional material supplied for exceptional line maintenance or small repair tasks; Report daily on the maintenance situation to the Chief Engineer. Base Maintenance Comply with the requirements as described in the regulations; Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the UK CAA and M.O.E. instructions; Ensure, together with all Engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business needs that supports & realizes business continuity; Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to ensure provision of serviceable aircraft to meet the operational need; Ensure briefing & debriefing of the Chief Engineer; Report daily on the maintenance situation; Inform and update the Chief Engineer on the maintenance situation; Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain, Store & support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. Participate in the daily morning Chief Engineer’s briefing (toolbox meeting).

item.title: Helicopter Hoist Operator - Licensed Engineer - Northern Europe
item.description: Are you an experienced helicopter hoist operator and licensed engineer with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights. The helicopter B1.3 or B2 Engineer/Hoist Operator is responsible for the maintenance (line) of the helicopters and execution of the hoist operations. He/she works in a team of engineers and pilots, and reports to the Base Manager. You will travel between bases in the Netherlands, Germany, Belgium, Denmark and Poland. Responsibilities & Areas Line Maintenance Comply with the requirements as described in the regulations; Ensure that all line maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and M.O.E. instructions; Ensure, together with all Engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business needs that supports & realizes business continuity; Ensure that all maintenance is completed within the appropriate time scales to ensure provision of a serviceable aircraft to meet the operational need; Ensure, based on pilot & all other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving; Ensure briefing & debriefing of pilots and Chief Engineer; Ensure that PRF (preflight check) is performed; Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain, Store & support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment: material in service car, consumables, limited service stock, work material, material needed for normal day-to-day line maintenance, exceptional material supplied for exceptional line maintenance or small repair tasks; Report daily on the maintenance situation to the Chief Engineer. HHO Helicopter Hoist operations Coordinate the passenger- and aircraft handling procedures before and after flights. Assist with the technical preparation of flights. Carrying out HHO flight duties, in accordance with NHV's SOP's related to the allocated helicopter type. Know the NHV procedures and the Flight Operations Manual. Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. Respecting the code of conduct and overall behavior towards the whole NHV team, as well as respecting the Drugs & Alcohol policy.

item.title: Deputy Chief Pilot (AW109, AW139 Rated)
item.description:
DOE
We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Deputy Chief Pilot to join our Flight Operations team. This role supports the Chief Pilot in the safe, efficient, and compliant delivery of all flying operations, while acting as a senior leader and mentor within the pilot group. The ideal candidate will bring strong operational experience, excellent communication skills, and the ability to support both strategic and day to day decision making within the department. Essential Requirements - AW109 Type Rating (current and valid) - Extensive operational experience in commercial rotary operations - Strong understanding of aviation regulations, compliance, and safety management - Excellent leadership, communication, and crew resource management skills - Proven ability to support operational planning, pilot oversight, and safety standards Desirable / Advantageous Qualifications - AW139 Type Rating - Multi Pilot rated and experienced - Previous experience in a senior flight operations or training role - Experience supporting SMS, compliance monitoring, or quality activities Key Responsibilities - Support the Chief Pilot in the management and oversight of all pilot operations - Act as deputy during absence of the Chief Pilot - Provide leadership, guidance, and support to the pilot team - Assist with pilot scheduling, rostering, and operational planning - Contribute to the Safety Management System, training oversight, and continuous improvement - Uphold the highest standards of safety, professionalism, and operational performance

item.title: B1.3 Licenced Engineer
item.description:
DOE
B1.3 Licenced Engineer – Kyle of Lochalsh Department: Engineering Location: Kyle of Lochalsh Closing Date: Open Salary: DOE Castle Air Ltd Licensed Engineer with Ground Support Responsibility Shift Pattern – 7 on 7 off 0r 14 days on 14 days off - Carry out both line and base maintenance at any of the Castle Air facilities, and also off-site upon customer demand. - Undertake all tasks related to aircraft maintenance - Ensure the highest quality and standards of work are completed in relation to aircraft, components and documentation. - Ensure facilities, equipment and premises are maintained to an acceptable standard - Ensuring operational crew members are debriefed to ensure defects are accurately defined and recorded - Analyse and interpret technical aircraft information - Work with other Castle Air Departments to ensure smooth operation of the business - Manage any stocks of consumables and parts that are on site in line with company procedures - Aircraft Towing/moving PERSON SPECIFICATION: Experience - Relevant experience and time on helicopters, time on the H145 is essential Skills - Excellent adaptable team worker, with good work and safety ethics. - Attention to Detail - Good interpersonal skills with ability to work within small team - Analytical approach to problem solving - Numerical Skills - Motivated to provide a high standard of work - Ability to consider the effects of their actions to both the Department and the Company as a whole. - Demonstrate integrity in all aspects - Good communication skills both written and verbal. - Have consideration for colleagues and customers and the ability to be inclusive to all others - Good IT skills and e the ability to learn new systems. We can offer a competitive salary, excellent benefits package and hanger facility. All applicants must be currently eligible to work and live in the UK.

item.title: Senior Supply Chain Manager
item.description:
DOE
Castle Air Aviation Group is seeking a highly experienced Senior Supply Chain Manager to lead and manage all procurement, stores and logistics functions across Castle Air UK and overseas bases, ensuring full regulatory compliance, cost-effective procurement, optimal stock availability, and strong operational support to maintenance and flight operations. About Castle Air: Castle Air has been a leader in rotary aviation for over 40 years, offering engineering, charter, and training services across the UK and Europe. We pride ourselves on our commitment to safety, innovation, and excellence. Key Responsibilities: - Lead, manage, and develop all Stores and Supply Chain staff, fostering teamwork and high performance. - Define departmental objectives, KPIs, and performance standards. - Ensure adequate resourcing, training (Aerotrac, regulatory), succession planning, and competency management. - Act as primary escalation point for supply chain issues (AOG, vendor failures, logistics delays). - Oversee all purchasing activities: parts, consumables, tooling, GSE, and contracted services. - Manage supplier selection, negotiation, contract management, and performance evaluation. - Develop procurement strategies to reduce cost, improve lead times, and manage risks. - Support aircraft teardown, part-out, storage, preservation, and part-sales processes. - Maintain strong relationships with OEMs, distributors, repair vendors, and partners. - Ensure all stores are fully Part 145 compliant, including storage conditions, segregation, and traceability. - Oversee tooling calibration programmes and compliance monitoring. - Develop stock level forecasting models and monitor stock accuracy, obsolescence, and rotable turnaround. - Ensure efficient import/export processes, customs compliance, and Dangerous Goods handling. - Lead AOG logistics support and oversee shipping, distribution, and dispatch standards. - Optimise use of Aerotrac (or other ERP/MRP) systems for inventory and logistics. - Drive continuous improvement initiatives and support ISO 9001:2015 compliance. - Maintain compliance with UK CAA/EASA Part 145, Part M, ADR/IATA DG, export control, and customs regulations. - Support audits, implement corrective actions, and improve procedures. - Act as key contact for supply chain matters with internal and external stakeholders. - Provide oversight for basic facilities support and coordinate third-party maintenance services. What You’ll Bring: - Proven experience in aviation supply chain and stores management. - Strong leadership and team development skills. - Excellent knowledge of regulatory compliance (UK CAA/EASA Part 145, Part M). - Skilled in procurement, vendor management, and contract negotiation. - Proficient in ERP/MRP systems (Aerotrac preferred). - Strong analytical, organisational, and communication skills. Requirements added by the job poster - Eligibility to work in UK - 8+ years of experience in Supply Chain

item.title: Helicopter Operations Assistant
item.description:
DOE
Castle Air is seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Operations Assistant to join our dynamic team. This role is pivotal in ensuring the safe and efficient operation of our daily flying programme while delivering exceptional service to our clients across the UK and Central Europe. As an Operations Assistant, you will: -Monitor and manage the daily flying schedule, including flight planning and active flight following. -Coordinate with internal departments such as CAMO and Maintenance for scheduled and unscheduled requirements. -Handle permits, third-party services, and ground handling arrangements. -Ensure compliance with company procedures, regulations, and safety standards. -Support emergency response planning and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives. What We’re Looking For: -Experience in aviation operations is desirable. -Strong scheduling or coordination experience is advantageous. -Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook). -Exceptional attention to detail, time management, and adaptability in a fast-paced environment. -Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with a professional approach. -Ability to work independently and as part of a team. -Full UK driving licence and legal right to work in the UK.

item.title: Production Planner
item.description:
DOE
Castle Air is seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Production Planner to join our team at Biggin Hill. In this role, you will be responsible for developing and managing production schedules to meet customer demands while ensuring optimal use of resources and maintaining high-quality standards. Key Responsibilities: Develop and maintain production schedules to meet customer requirements and deadlines. Coordinate with internal departments to ensure smooth production flow. Monitor inventory levels and adjust schedules as needed. Implement continuous improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency. Compile and analyse data to create comprehensive production plans. Utilise scheduling systems to track and report progress. Collaborate with the supply chain team for timely material delivery. Oversee quality control measures throughout the production process. Qualifications: Minimum GCSEs (Grade 4/C or above) in English and Maths. A-Level standard education or equivalent vocational qualification. Strong organisational skills with the ability to multitask effectively. Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Word for data management and reporting. Why Join Castle Air? Work with a leading aviation company committed to excellence and innovation. Competitive salary and benefits package. Opportunity to contribute to a dynamic and growing team.

item.title: B2 Licenced Engineer
item.description:
DOE
We are seeking a B2 Licensed Aircraft Engineer to join our team at our facility at Boscombe Down. Castle Air has a 40 year heritage in the rotary field, and is already well known for maintaining Leonardo aircraft. Applicants will be asked to provide proof of recency and must be able to demonstrate the right to work in the UK. Duties include but not limited to: - Provide and communicate regular engineering reports to the Head of Maintenance/ Head of Department - Certify work within the scope of company authorisations - Carry out both line and base maintenance at any of the Castle Air facilities, and also off-site upon customer demand. - Undertake all tasks related to aircraft maintenance - Ensure the highest quality and standards of work are completed in relation to aircraft, components and documentation. - Ensure facilities, equipment and premises are maintained to an acceptable standard - Ensuring operational crew members are debriefed to ensure defects are accurately defined and recorded - Analyse and interpret technical aircraft information - Work with other Castle Air Departments to ensure smooth operation of the business - Ensure compliance is maintained to industry requirements and authorities (such as the CAA; EASA etc.) - Comply with all Continuing Professional Development requirements relevant to the relevant company policies and reporting authority. - Complete all tasks as specified in an efficient and compliant manner ensuring that safety matters are always considered and adhered to. - Effective management of all maintenance within your control. Benefits: We can offer a competitive salary, excellent benefits package and hanger facility. - 31 days annual leave including bank holidays - Additional day to celebrate your birthday - Additional holiday between Boxing Day and New Years Up to 5% employer pension contribution (online with current legislation) - Employee funded private health scheme, option to include family members - Income Protection Cover All applicants must be currently eligible to work and live in the UK, and have the ability to hold a security clearance. Please click apply or submit your CV and covering letter to recruitment@castleair.co.uk Please note that due to the volume of applications we are unable to respond individually to all applications. Candidates who have been successfully shortlisted for interview will be contacted directly. We would like to thank all applicants for the time and effort taken in applying.

item.title: Fixed Wing Pilot
item.description:
Base Pay Rate: $61.92 - $78.72 per hour based on experience
Job Application for Fixed Wing Pilot at Life Link III Back to jobs Fixed Wing Pilot Anoka, MN Apply Who Are We: Life Link III is a premier, nationally recognized air medical transport company, known for our clinical excellence, superior aviation program and reliable response in providing care to critically ill or injured patients. Our priority is to do everything possible to give every patient their best chance for the best possible outcome - demonstrated by our unwavering commitment to providing the highest level of care to our patients while on board safe, state-of-the-art aircraft. We continually focus on innovation and work to put cutting-edge technology and education in the hands of our flight medical crew, pilots, mechanics, operational control specialists and communication specialists so we can provide uncompromising care to the communities we serve. We do this work while living out our core values of safety, customer focus, excellence, integrity, innovation, and collaboration. As a Fixed-Wing Pilot, you’ll play a critical role in transporting patients and medical crews safely and efficiently, supporting life-saving care with every flight. Flying our Pilatus PC-12 NG from the Anoka base, you’ll partner closely with your medical team to ensure each transport is conducted with the highest standards of safety, compliance, and operational excellence. Your Impact : Final authority for the safety of passengers, cargo, and medical personnel, and has operational control for all flights which they initiate. Accomplishes pre-flight inspections. Conducts all flight operations in compliance with the FARs, the aircraft flight manual, the General Operations Manual, Operations Specifications, and Life Link III’s policies/procedures. Ensures full and proper completion of all flight documentation. Maintains a positive attitude with both fellow employees and customers. Able to conduct detailed flight planning and weather checks with little notice. Responsible for pertinent information regarding all flights, including NOTAMs, TFRs, and weather information. Responsible for maintaining qualifications as required in FAR’s. Follows and upholds the principles of CRM (Crew Resource Management) Supervises loading of passengers, baggage, and fuel and determines that weight and balance remains within the limitations contained in the aircraft flight manual for all flight operations (Part 91 and 135). Performs other duties as assigned. Your Qualifications Commercial pilot certificate & Instrument Rating for category and class of aircraft. (Airplane, Single Engine Land) (ATP in category and class meets this requirement.) First or Second-Class FAA Medical Certificate 2500 Total time 1000 hours PIC in category 500 hours of cross-country 100 hours nighttime 100 hours of actual instrument time in flight in category. Able to work cooperatively and collaboratively with all levels of employees, management, and external entities to maximize performance, creativity, problem-solving, and results. Travel between bases as needed Preferred Qualifications: 3000 hours total time 2000 hours aircraft flight hours in category 300 hours of Turbine time. Previous PC-12 experience Experience in HAA operations Bachelor’s degree Prior experience in Part 135 or Part 121 operation ATP Single pilot experience Type Rated Dual rated Winter flying experience in Great Lakes Region Prior check pilot experience What We Offer: Base Pay Rate: $61.92 - $78.72 per hour based on experience Pilot Retention Bonus Program Structure & Payouts Eligible pilots can earn a total of $45,000 over three consecutive years of service. Payouts are structured as follows and are contingent upon meeting the service requirements for each 12-month period and will be paid out at the end of each 12-month period. Year 1: $10,000, Year 2: $15,000, Year 3: $20,000 Total: $45,000 Health, Dental, and Vision insurance coverage starting the first of the month following date of hire Health Savings Account (HSA) with employer match 401k with employer match fully vested Generous paid leave and paid time off to foster work/life balance Life, disability, critical illness, and accident insurance Tuition/Training reimbursement program 12-hour shifts with a 7 on 7 off schedule Annual $100 boot reimbursement Company use of aircraft to obtain ATP Why Join Life Link III? We are dedicated to making a difference. Our mission-driven culture fosters innovation, collaboration, and professional growth. You'll work alongside passionate professionals in a CAMTS accredited, award-winning program that is committed to improving patient outcomes all while advancing your career in a supportive environment. Compliance/HIPAA: Must perform work in the manner prescribed by documented work process in order to ensure compliance with all rules and regulations, accreditation standards and laws that apply, specifically work processes that involve billing practices, for avoiding activities that constitute fraud and abuse. Employment Status Requirements: Life Link III does not sponsor employment visas of any kind. As a pre-condition of employment, all applicants must be authorized to work in the United States. Life Link III is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where all individuals are treated with respect and dignity. We are an Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) employer and believe in providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, veteran status, marital or family status, status with regard to public assistance, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. Our commitment to an equitable workplace extends to all aspects of employment, including recruitment, hiring, promotions, transfers, training, working conditions, compensation, benefits, and termination. We are dedicated to maintaining a workplace that is free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation, and where all individuals can thrive professionally. If you require any accommodations to apply or participate in our hiring process, please contact the Life Link III H uman R esources team.

item.title: Flight Paramedic - Resource
item.description:
Base Pay Rate: $34.15 - $40.16 per hour, based on experience
Job Application for Flight Paramedic - Resource at Life Link III Back to jobs Flight Paramedic - Resource Anoka, MN Apply Who Are We: Life Link III is a premier, nationally recognized air medical transport company, known for our clinical excellence, superior aviation program and reliable response in providing care to critically ill or injured patients. Our priority is to do everything possible to give every patient their best chance for the best possible outcome - demonstrated by our unwavering commitment to providing the highest level of care to our patients while on board safe, state-of-the-art aircraft. We continually focus on innovation and work to put cutting-edge technology and education in the hands of our flight medical crew, pilots, mechanics, operational control specialists and communication specialists so we can provide uncompromising care to the communities we serve. We do this work while living out our core values of safety, customer focus, excellence, integrity, innovation, and collaboration. As a Critical Care Flight Paramedic at Life Link III, you’ll join an elite team of flight nurses and pilots, delivering advanced life-saving care with top-of-the-line equipment in air medical transport. Experience a high level of autonomy as you assess and stabilize critically ill or injured patients, performing advanced procedures and ensuring their safe journey to specialized medical facilities. This fast-paced, rewarding role demands expert critical care knowledge, mastery of complex interventions, and exceptional communication—where every mission makes a difference. Your Impact : Provide advanced critical care to patients with life-threatening conditions during air medical transport Assess, stabilize, and manage trauma, cardiac, respiratory, and pediatric emergencies in flight Perform advanced procedures within paramedic scope Administer medications, IV fluids, and blood products per guidelines and established protocols Ensure safety throughout all phases of transport in collaboration with pilot and flight nurse Collaborate closely with flight nurses, pilots, and other professionals to ensure optimal patient outcomes during transport Maintains clear and continuous communication with receiving hospitals, pre-hospital care providers, and family members as necessary Participate in pre-flight safety checks, post flight debriefings and maintain equipment readiness Document patient care accurately and support quality improvement initiatives Maintain critical care skills through ongoing training Your Qualifications Current Paramedic license in MN, WI, with critical care endorsement required and preferred ND and MI licensures BLS, ACLS, PALS certifications required; trauma certifications (TPATC) are encouraged FP-C will be required within 18 months of hire date NRP (Neonatal Resuscitation Program) certification will be required after hire date FEMA certification for ICS 100, 200 A minimum of 3 years of recent (within the last 5 years) critical care of 911 EMS paramedic experience with a focus on high-acuity, critical patient care Prior critical care experience that involved a comprehensive understanding of advanced life support techniques, pharmacological therapies, invasive procedures, and emergency interventions Prior exposure to high-acuity, multi-system trauma, including shock, severe head injuries, and complex cardiac emergencies, is essential Must maintain a flight crew weight of 230 lbs or less, fully dressed in flight required gear Valid driver’s license Ability to maintain airport security access credentials (where applicable) Preferred Qualifications: Previous flight experience preferred Experience with adult and pediatric patient populations, including neonates, highly preferred What We Offer: Base Pay Rate: $34.15 - $40.16 per hour, based on experience Health, Dental, and Vision insurance coverage starting the first of the month following date of hire Health Savings Account (HSA) with employer match 401k with employer match fully vested Generous paid leave and paid time off to foster work/life balance Life, disability, critical illness, and accident insurance Tuition/Training reimbursement program Why Join Life Link III? We are dedicated to making a difference. Our mission-driven culture fosters innovation, collaboration, and professional growth. You'll work alongside passionate professionals in a CAMTS accredited, award-winning program that is committed to improving patient outcomes all while advancing your career in a supportive environment. Job Application for Flight Registered Nurse at Life Link III Back to jobs Flight Registered Nurse Hibbing, MN Apply Who Are We: Life Link III is a premier, nationally recognized air medical transport company, known for our clinical excellence, superior aviation program and reliable response in providing care to critically ill or injured patients. item.title: Helicopter Line Mechanic
item.description:
Base Pay Rate: $39.33 - $54.44 per hour, based on experience
Job Application for Helicopter Line Mechanic at Life Link III Back to jobs Helicopter Line Mechanic Willmar, MN Apply Who Are We: Life Link III is a premier, nationally recognized air medical transport company, known for our clinical excellence, superior aviation program and reliable response in providing care to critically ill or injured patients. Our priority is to do everything possible to give every patient their best chance for the best possible outcome - demonstrated by our unwavering commitment to providing the highest level of care to our patients while on board safe, state-of-the-art aircraft. We continually focus on innovation and work to put cutting-edge technology and education in the hands of our flight medical crew, pilots, mechanics, operational control specialists and communication specialists so we can provide uncompromising care to the communities we serve. We do this work while living out our core values of safety, customer focus, excellence, integrity, innovation, and collaboration. At Life Link III, every flight matters. Our Helicopter Line Mechanics play a critical role in ensuring our aircraft are always mission-ready—so our clinical crews can safely deliver lifesaving care when communities need it most. If you’re an experienced A&P who values safety, precision, and teamwork, this is your opportunity to make a real impact while working on a state-of-the-art helicopter fleet. As a Helicopter Line Mechanic, you’ll support Life Link III’s mission by maintaining and repairing aircraft to the highest safety and regulatory standards. Your work ensures reliability, compliance, and readiness across our operations. Your Impact : Performing inspections, maintenance, and repairs on aircraft structures, mechanical, and electronic systems Ensuring all maintenance documentation meets Federal Aviation Regulations (FARs) , company policies, and technical manuals Verifying part and serial number accuracy during component installation and removal Supporting our Safety Management System through active participation and continuous improvement Maintaining a clean, organized, and professional work environment Identifying and correcting compliance or safety concerns Communicating aircraft status and maintenance issues to the Pilot in Command (PIC) Validating aircraft status reports and requesting parts to support operational readiness Your Qualifications High School diploma or GED FAA A&P license in good standing 2–5 years of related aviation maintenance experience Minimum 2 years of rotorcraft experience as an A&P Mechanic FAR Part 135 experience (preferred) Factory airframe and/or powerplant training (preferred) ERP experience (preferred) Proficiency with Microsoft Office Valid driver’s license Ownership of basic required maintenance tools Ability to live within one hour of the assigned base What We Offer: Base Pay Rate: $39.33 - $54.44 per hour, based on experience Health, Dental, and Vision insurance coverage starting the first of the month following date of hire Health Savings Account (HSA) with employer match 401k with employer match fully vested Generous paid leave and paid time off to foster work/life balance Life, disability, critical illness, and accident insurance Tuition/Training reimbursement program Why Join Life Link III? We are dedicated to making a difference. Our mission-driven culture fosters innovation, collaboration, and professional growth. You'll work alongside passionate professionals in a CAMTS accredited, award-winning program that is committed to improving patient outcomes all while advancing your career in a supportive environment. Compliance/HIPAA: Must perform work in the manner prescribed by documented work process in order to ensure compliance with all rules and regulations, accreditation standards and laws that apply, specifically work processes that involve billing practices, for avoiding activities that constitute fraud and abuse. Employment Status Requirements: Life Link III does not sponsor employment visas of any kind. As a pre-condition of employment, all applicants must be authorized to work in the United States. Life Link III is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where all individuals are treated with respect and dignity. We are an Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) employer and believe in providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, veteran status, marital or family status, status with regard to public assistance, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. item.title: Relief Helicopter Line Mechanic (East Region)
item.description:
$39.33 - $54.44 per hour, based on experience
Who Are We: Life Link III is a premier, nationally recognized air medical transport company, known for our clinical excellence, superior aviation program and reliable response in providing care to critically ill or injured patients. Our priority is to do everything possible to give every patient their best chance for the best possible outcome - demonstrated by our unwavering commitment to providing the highest level of care to our patients while on board safe, state-of-the-art aircraft. We continually focus on innovation and work to put cutting-edge technology and education in the hands of our flight medical crew, pilots, mechanics, operational control specialists and communication specialists so we can provide uncompromising care to the communities we serve. We do this work while living out our core values of safety, customer focus, excellence, integrity, innovation, and collaboration. As a Relief Helicopter Line Mechanic you will provide maintenance support across multiple Life Link III bases as operational needs require. This role is designed for a highly adaptable A&P who can work independently, transition between bases and aircraft types, and maintain aircraft to the highest airworthiness and safety standards. Your Impact: Perform aircraft inspections, maintenance, and repairs in accordance with FAA regulations, airworthiness directives, service bulletins, and manufacturer manuals Troubleshoot and resolve mechanical, structural, and electronic aircraft issues Ensure all maintenance documentation is accurate, complete, and compliant Work autonomously at remote bases while collaborating with pilots, medical crew, and base leadership Advise the Pilot in Command (PIC) of maintenance issues that may impact aircraft readiness Validate aircraft status reports and maintain accurate electronic maintenance logs Order parts and supplies to support operational readiness Maintain tools, equipment, and work areas in a clean, safe, and professional condition Actively participate in Life Link III’s Safety Program and continuous improvement efforts Learn new aircraft types and base-specific procedures as needed Support safe, efficient flight operations—every mission, every time Perform other duties as assigned Your Qualifications: High School diploma or GED FAA A&P license in good standing 2–5 years of related aircraft maintenance experience and/or training Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite Experience with electronic maintenance log systems (e.g., Veryon, Flight Docs, Complete Flight, RAMCO, etc.) Must own tools required for basic maintenance Strong troubleshooting, problem-solving, and communication skills Ability to read and interpret aircraft maintenance manuals and technical publications Ability to work flexible schedules, including overtime and 24/7 on-call rotations Ability to travel a minimum of 60% to Life Link III bases and locations Must live within one hour of the assigned Life Link III base Valid driver’s license Ability to meet the physical demands of the role, including lifting up to 70 lbs., climbing, prolonged standing, and working in confined spaces Preferred Qualifications Factory airframe and/or powerplant training FAR Part 135 experience ERP system experience Inspection Authorization (IA) What We Offer: Base Pay Rate: $39.33 - $54.44 per hour, based on experience Health, Dental, and Vision insurance coverage starting the first of the month following date of hire Health Savings Account (HSA) with employer match 401k with employer match fully vested Generous paid leave and paid time off to foster work/life balance Life, disability, critical illness, and accident insurance Tuition/Training reimbursement program Why Join Life Link III?
