item.title: Rotor Wing Pilot
item.description:
Base Pay Rate: $53.74 - $67.11 per hour, based on experience
Job Application for Rotor Wing Pilot at Life Link III Back to jobs Rotor Wing Pilot Rice Lake, WI Apply Who Are We: Life Link III is a premier, nationally recognized air medical transport company, known for our clinical excellence, superior aviation program and reliable response in providing care to critically ill or injured patients. Our priority is to do everything possible to give every patient their best chance for the best possible outcome - demonstrated by our unwavering commitment to providing the highest level of care to our patients while on board safe, state-of-the-art aircraft. We continually focus on innovation and work to put cutting-edge technology and education in the hands of our flight medical crew, pilots, mechanics, operational control specialists and communication specialists so we can provide uncompromising care to the communities we serve. We do this work while living out our core values of safety, customer focus, excellence, integrity, innovation, and collaboration. As a Rotor Wing Pilot at Life Link III, you’ll be part of an elite air medical team, flying state-of-the-art aircraft to transport medical crews and patients to the care they need. You’ll play a vital role in every mission, ensuring safe and professional flights while navigating complex aviation regulations and company procedures. In this position, you’ll combine your expert piloting skills with a commitment to safety, teamwork, and making a real difference for patients and their families. Your Impact : Responsible for safety of passengers and crew, and oversees all flights accepted Effectively communicate and collaborate with flight crew, dispatch and partners Actively shares flight knowledge with team members and serves as a resource to discuss transport, flight operations, and other work-related topics Follows all regulations, manuals, and company policies during flights Completes flight documentation accurately and promptly Supervises loading of aircraft, refueling and ensures safe weight and balance Adheres to proper Crew Resources Management (CRM) technique Actively participates in the Life Link III Safety Management System Responsible for pertinent information regarding all flights, including NOTAMs, TFRs, and weather information Work cohesively as a unit to accomplish all Life Link III assignments including patient transfers Responsible for maintaining qualifications as required in CFRs Proactively pursues professional growth and supports the learning and development of others Your Qualifications Commercial pilot certificate and instrument rating for category and class of aircraft Current FAA Class I or II Medical Certificate 2000 total flight hours or meets CAMTS equivalency 1500 hours Helicopter flight hours 1000 hours PIC in category 500 hours of rotorcraft turbine time 200 hours of cross country 50 hours of unaided night can be accepted with a minimum of 100 hours of aided night 50 hours of hood or actual time instrument time in flight in category. (Simulator time does not count.) A pilot that is dual qualified in fixed wing with 100 hours of actual instrument time can reduce this requirement to 25 hours. Able to work cooperatively and collaboratively with all Preferred Qualifications: Prior experience in HAA operations Prior Search and Rescue Experience ATP Certificate Bachelor’s degree What We Offer: Base Pay Rate: $53.74 - $67.11 per hour, based on experience Health, Dental, and Vision insurance coverage starting the first of the month following date of hire Health Savings Account (HSA) with employer match 401k with employer match fully vested Generous paid leave and paid time off to foster work/life balance Life, disability, critical illness, and accident insurance Tuition/Training reimbursement program 12-hour shifts with a 7 on 7 off schedule Annual $100 boot reimbursement Company use of aircraft to obtain ATP Why Join Life Link III? We are dedicated to making a difference. Our mission-driven culture fosters innovation, collaboration, and professional growth. You'll work alongside passionate professionals in a CAMTS accredited, award-winning program that is committed to improving patient outcomes all while advancing your career in a supportive environment. Compliance/HIPAA: Must perform work in the manner prescribed by documented work process in order to ensure compliance with all rules and regulations, accreditation standards and laws that apply, specifically work processes that involve billing practices, for avoiding activities that constitute fraud and abuse. Employment Status Requirements: Life Link III does not sponsor employment visas of any kind. As a pre-condition of employment, all applicants must be authorized to work in the United States. We are an Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) employer and believe in providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, veteran status, marital or family status, status with regard to public assistance, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. item.title: Capitaine d'hélicoptère / Premier officier (Opérations d'ambulance aérienne)
Helicopter Captain / First Officer (Air Ambulance Operations)
item.description:
Competitive salary according to experience.
Join our elite crew and embark on an exciting career path within our industry. As one of top leaders in the industry, we have an exciting opportunity for a Captain and First Officer on the S76, in support of our Air Ambulance Operations in Halifax, Nova Scotia. If you are looking to further your career, we invite you to apply today and be a part of our growing team! Responsibilities Captain : Act as a Captain as per Company operation standards; Perform IFR Flights in challenging and rapidly changing environment; Be able to work closely with our customer, respond efficiently to aircraft tasking, while maintaining a high level of safety; Coordination and effective communication between crew and dispatch All duties assigned by the Chief Pilot or Base Manager; First Officer: Act as a pilot as per Company operation standards; Any other responsibility related to the position Qualifications Common requirements Must be willing to relocate to Halifax, Nova Scotia. A valid Canadian passport or a valid authorization to work in Canada and to travel to USA, (this position is not available for sponsorship); Valid Class 1 Medical; Ability to successfully complete the Controlled Goods Security assessment application for NVG purposes; Immunization records with required vaccinations are mandatory. Be customer focused and a team player; Have good communication skills and a positive attitude; A Criminal record search will be conducted prior to hire. Requirements specific by position Captain: A valid and unrestricted Transport Canada ATPL(H) License; A minimum of 2000 hours rotary wing flight time; A minimum of 500 hours as Pilot-in-Command (PIC) which 200 hours of Multi-Engine Aircraft; Minimum of 2 years of experience in Instrument Flight Operations with actual IFR flight hours as PIC; Previous experience flying in a 703 Air Ambulance operation as a Captain is preferred; NVG experience an asset; Endorsement on S76 considered an asset; First Officer: Have a valid ATPL or have passed ATPL written exams within the previous 12 months; Hold a valid Commercial Rotary-wing License; Have 500 hours Rotary-wing time; Transport Canada Group IV Instrument rating; Possess unrestricted Night Rating; Possess previous Night / IFR flying experience in the Maritime environment is an asset. Additional Information Remuneration: Competitive salary according to experience. Schedule : Shifts schedule. Not a touring position as you will be based out of Halifax, NS. Working for Canadian Helicopters is: A unique occasion to grow professionally in a dynamic environment full of opportunities. Working with passionate, committed people who enjoy surpassing themselves. Being part of an organizational culture based on fundamental principles. Having an employer of choice offering competitive working conditions. We offer a flexible benefits program including comprehensive medical and dental coverage, life insurance, short and long term disability insurance and a pension plan, depending on employment status. Canadian Helicopters Limited is committed to employment equity and diversity and encourages applications from women, Indigenous people, and visible minorities. For more information we invite you to visit our website: www.canadianhelicopters.com Follow us on LinkedIn: https://ca.linkedin.com/company/canadian-helicopters-limited

item.title: Materials Department Coordinator
item.description: This individual will report directly to the Material Manager and will be responsible for various material department administrative functions. Specific duties include, but are not limited to: Assist with inventory taking and update system data based on actual counts. Shall issue/receive parts to/from aircraft using the base forms and aircrafts log pages. Shall create and update supplier data in Airsoft. Qualifications Must hold the authorization to work in Canada (this position is not available for sponsorship). This position requires access to Controlled Goods; therefore, the successful candidate must be eligible to qualify under the Controlled Goods Program. Excellent organization and communication skills. Ability to manage and adapt to changing priorities. Good computer skills (Knowledge of Airsoft is an asset). Ability to work within a group environment. Good communication skills in both English and French (As a pan-Canadian company, bilingualism is required to ensure communications between all bases across the country). Additional Information Status: Permanent, full-time. Location: Les Cèdres, QC. 100% in person Schedule: Monday-Friday, 40 hours per week Working for Canadian Helicopters is: A unique opportunity to evolve in a dynamic environment full of opportunities. Work with passionate, committed people who like to surpass themselves. Be part of an organizational culture based on fundamental principles. Having an employer of choice offering competitive working conditions. We offer a flexible benefits program including comprehensive medical and dental coverage, life insurance, short and long term disability insurance and a pension fund plan. Canadian Helicopters Limited is committed to employment equity and diversity and encourages applications from women, Aboriginal peoples, persons with disabilities and visible minorities.

item.title: Aeronautical Components Technician
item.description: The Aeronautical Components Technician reports directly to the Engine and Components Overhaul Shop Manager. He is responsible for the inspection, repair or overhaul of aeronautical components. He ensures that the execution of his tasks is carried out in accordance with the airworthiness standards established by the manufacturer, the Canadian Aviation Regulations as well as those of the company. Responsibilities : Perform maintenance activities such as, arrival inspections, disassembly, cleaning, inspection, repair and assembly of aeronautical components; Keep personal qualifications up to date; Adhere to the standards, procedures and policies established by the company; Use various software and files related to the execution of tasks. Qualifications Diploma in aircraft mechanical assembly or aircraft maintenance technician diploma (completed or in the process of being completed) OR experience in the overhaul and repair of helicopter components or turboshaft engines. Bilingualism (French and English, spoken and written). As a pan-Canadian company, bilingualism is required to ensure communications between all bases across the country. Basic knowledge of MS Office Suite; Ability to work in a team; Analytical mind, problem-solving skills and organizational skills. Additional Information Location: 1215 Montée-Pilon, Les Cèdres, Qc. Status: permanent, full time Hours: Monday to Friday from 8am to 5pm Working for Canadian Helicopters means: Having a unique opportunity to develop professionally in a dynamic environment full of opportunities; Working with passionate, committed people who like to excel; Being part of an organizational culture based on fundamental principles; Having an employer of choice offering competitive working conditions. We offer a flexible benefits program including comprehensive medical and dental coverage, life insurance, short and long term disability insurance and a pension fund plan. Canadian Helicopters Limited is committed to employment equity and diversity and encourages applications from women, Indigenous peoples, persons with disabilities and visible minorities.

item.title: Pilote d'hélicoptère (Emplacements multiples)
Technicien d’entretien d’aéronefs (Plusieurs emplacements)
item.description:
Salaire compétitif selon expérience.
Technicien d’entretien d’aéronefs (Plusieurs emplacements) Temps plein Location de la base: Autre Description de l'entreprise Hélicoptères Canadiens est une entreprise de services de transport par hélicoptère, de soutien à la maintenance et de formation au pilotage. En affaires depuis 1947, Hélicoptères Canadiens est le plus grand exploitant d'hélicoptères au Canada avec plus de 20 emplacements à travers le pays et une flotte de près de 100 appareils monomoteurs et multimoteurs. Description du poste Les tâches du TEA comprennent: Effectuer l’entretien des aéronefs et la planification; S’assurer que toutes les pièces de rechange sont inspectées et correctement certifiées avant l’installation; Participer à l’organisation et à la bonne tenue du hangar; Assurer le respect de toutes les politiques et procédures de l’entreprise; Se déplacer vers divers lieux de travail et environnements au besoin; Effectuer des tâches connexes assignées par le superviseur. Plusieurs sites disponibles: Southport (MB) Pool Across Canada Qualifications Détenir une licence de Transport Canada M1 et/ou M2 valide; Détenir le droit légal de travailler au Canada, ce poste n'est pas disponible pour un parrainage; Détenir, avoir détenu ou être admissible à détenir une autorisation certifiée en aéronefs (ACA) sur un ou plusieurs des appareils suivants : AS350B2 AS350B3 AS355N Série B206 B212 B412 S61 note: détenir une ACA sur d'autres types d'avions CHL serait un atout certain; Sens de l’organisation et initiative; Orientation service client, bonne aptitude au niveau de la communication, attitude positive et esprit d'équipe; Disponible pour faire des tours à l’extérieur de la base selon les besoins du client. Capacité à naviguer et à travailler efficacement dans un environnement Windows. Il peut être nécessaire d’obtenir une autorisation d’accès aux marchandises contrôlées avec l’approbation de l’entreprise. Selon le lieu d'affectation, des qualifications supplémentaires pourraient être requises, telles que : Avoir réussi un cours de maintenance de base dispensé par un organisme de formation agréé par Transports Canada, Aviation civile (TCAC) ou par le MDN; - Southport (MB) Les candidats possédant une qualification en maintenance d’aéronefs militaires canadiens seront considérés. - Southport (MB) Informations supplémentaires Rémuneration: Salaire compétitif selon expérience. Travailler pour Hélicoptères Canadiens, c’est : Une opportunité unique d'évoluer professionnellement dans un environnement dynamique et plein d'opportunités ; Travailler avec des personnes passionnées, engagées et qui aiment se dépasser ; Faire partie d'une culture organisationnelle basée sur des principes fondamentaux; Avoir un employeur de choix offrant des conditions de travail compétitives. Nous offrons un programme flexible d'avantages sociaux incluant une protection médicale et dentaire complète, une assurance vie, une assurance invalidité de courte et de longue durée et un régime de fonds de pension, selon le statut d'emploi. Hélicoptères Canadiens Limitée souscrit à l’équité en matière d’emploi et la diversité et encourage les candidatures venant des femmes, des Autochtones, des personnes handicapées et des minorités visibles. Pour de plus amples informations, nous vous invitons à visiter notre site web à l’adresse https://www.canadianhelicopters.com/fr/ Suivez-nous sur LinkedIn : https://ca.linkedin.com/company/canadian-helicopters-limited Je suis intéressé(e) Recommander un(e) ami(e) partager ce poste Partager sur WeChat × Copiez le lien et ouvrez WeChat pour partager. Copier dans le presse-papier Ouvrir WeChat Utilisez Scan QR Code dans WeChat et cliquez sur --- pour partager.

item.title: Capitaine d'hélicoptère
Technicien d’entretien d’aéronefs
item.description:
Salaire compétitif selon expérience.
Le TEA relèvera du Superviseur du hangar et ses tâches reliées à ce poste incluent : Effectuer l’entretien des aéronefs et la planification; S’assurer que toutes les pièces de rechange sont inspectées et correctement certifiées avant l’installation; Participer à l’organisation et à la bonne tenue du hangar; Assurer le respect de toutes les politiques et procédures de l’entreprise; Se déplacer vers divers lieux de travail et environnements au besoin; Effectuer des tâches connexes assignées par le superviseur. Qualifications Détenir une licence de Transport Canada M1 et/ou M2 valide; Détenir le droit légal de travailler au Canada, ce poste n'est pas disponible pour un parrainage; Détenir une autorisation certifiée en aéronefs (ACA) sur les appareils AS350B2 et/ou AS355N et/ou BH206; Une autorisation certifiée en aéronefs sur autres types d’appareils opérés par HCL sera considérée comme un atout; Sens de l’organisation et initiative; Orientation service client, bonne aptitude au niveau de la communication et attitude positive; Doit habiter dans la région de Sept-Îles ou être prêt à déménager dans la région; Disponible pour faire des tours à l’extérieur de la base selon les besoins de notre client.

item.description: Emergency Medicine Careers at Geisinger Skip to main content Need help? Email geisingercareers@geisinger.edu Login Current employees Saved jobs 0 There are no jobs saved About us Who we are Accessibility & Opportunity Locations Veterans Working here Career development Benefits Candidate resources Hiring policies Students High School College Physicians Physicians Homepage About Geisinger Lifestyle Connect With Us Accolades Anesthesiology Community Medicine Emergency Medicine Geisinger Cancer Institute Hospital Medicine Psychiatry Nursing Nursing Homepage Why nursing at Geisinger Experienced RNs & LPNs Travel RN Recently graduated RNs Nursing students Nursing support Advanced practice Hospitality Services Environmental Services Foodservices Other roles Advanced Practice Dietitian Emergency Medical Services Lab Pharmacy Radiology Social Work All jobs A place for providers to help communities and shape the future of healthcare. Only at Geisinger. Search Open Career Opportunities Keyword Search City, State, or ZIP Search radius Radius 5 miles 15 miles 25 miles 35 miles 50 miles Search Emergency Medicine Opportunities At Geisinger, we're not afraid to do things differently when it comes to delivering excellent care. That spirit of innovation and excellence drives everything we do – from our state-of-the-art emergency medical services program, to our new and expanding graduate medical education programs, to our progressive care models that focus on preventive care. These are just some of the ways we go above and beyond to help the communities we serve. To stay up to date on all things Geisinger Emergency Medicine, connect with us on LinkedIn Our Teams Advanced Practice Providers A vital part of our care team, Geisinger’s Emergency Medicine APPs can expect the best development resources and training to thrive in their role. Geisinger’s Department of Advanced Practice Providers serves to support our Advanced Practitioners and Advanced Practitioner students through interdepartmental consultation, benchmarking of best practices, professional practice standards, compensation, personnel management, and care delivery models. In addition to a comprehensive range of benefits, candidates also receive a $20k recruitment incentive to join our team. Pediatric Emergency Medicine Our team is bringing top-notch Pediatric Emergency Medicine to rural communities across central Pennsylvania through an innovative approach to pediatric readiness. Our regional strategy leverages Pediatric Emergency Medicine specialist knowledge and skills to support our outstanding Emergency Medicine colleagues in caring for children across our 10-hospital system. We use telemedicine, clinical guideline development with electronic health record integration, pediatric equipment standardization, clinical tool development, education and simulation initiatives, participation in national collaboratives, local and regional quality improvement, an amazing system Pediatric Emergency Care Coordinator, and a regional, multidisciplinary Pediatric Emergency Care Coordinator (PECC) council to help deliver high quality care at diverse emergency departments across a large geographic distribution. Central to the coordination of pediatric emergency care within our system is the colorful, 9-bed “Peds Zone” in the Emergency Department at the Janet Weis Children’s Hospital at Geisinger Medical Center (Pediatric Emergency Room | Geisinger) in Danville, PA. The “Peds Zone” will be expanding to a 14-bed unit in the next few years with the completion of a critical care tower expansion project (GMC Expansion | Geisinger | News Release). The Janet Weis Children’s Hospital (Janet Weis Children’s Hospital & CHOP | Geisinger) at Geisinger Medical Center is a 90-bed children’s hospital with a 14-bed PICU and 41-bed NICU that provides a comprehensive range of pediatric services including a full array of medical and surgical pediatric subspecialties. We are also establishing a second hub for Pediatric Emergency Medicine at the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Hospital Emergency Department in Wilkes-Barre, PA. In addition to providing outstanding bedside care and coordinating pediatric emergency care throughout our system, our team is very involved in resident education, national collaboratives, and research. We work closely as faculty to teach and mentor Pediatrics, Med/Peds, Emergency Medicine Residents, and medical students through the Geisinger Commonwealth Medical School (Geisinger College of Health Sciences). Recent Geisinger PEM faculty accomplishments include: EMSC Scholars appointment (Meet the 2022 EMSC Scholars - EIIC) EMSC Fellowship appointment (Steve "Jason" Crellin, DO - EIIC) 2024 Amanda Wertz Memorial Emergency Medical Services for Children Award (Geisinger pediatric emergency medicine physician receives award) Pediatric Sepsis Emergency Criteria Expert Panel Membership Geisinger Academy of Educators Distinguished membership (Academy of Educators | Geisinger College of Health Sciences) GCSOM Medical Student Summer Research Immersion Program mentorship (Summer Research Immersion Program | Geisinger Commonwealth) Academic presentations at Pediatric Academic Society, Pennsylvania ACEP, The American Medical Informatics Association Journal Publications through the American Journal of Emergency Medicine, Pediatrics Textbook chapter authorship in Fleischer Ludwig and Pediatric Critical Care Study Guide Ultrasound Collaboration and knowledge sharing are top-of-mind here. We offer system-wide clinical ultrasound administration opportunities, where everyone can collaborate across departments. There’s also a monthly multidisciplinary image review conference with different departments like Critical Care, Cardiology, and Sports Medicine. Our teams conduct weekly QA of images obtained in the emergency department, used to develop ultrasound quality improvement projects. Other perks include a one-year Accredited Emergency Ultrasound Fellowship within the Emergency Ultrasound Fellowship Accreditation Council, based at GMC (Danville, PA), as well as archiving and quality assurance services. Research Our research team works closely with the EM residency program leadership to improve resident training and foster healthy intellectual dialogue. In 2023, our Emergency Department published 21 peer-reviewed articles and three book chapters/textbooks and presented our work at 70 scientific conferences. System-wide, we had >$57 million in external grant and contract revenue, >1,400 active research projects, including clinical trials, and >1,090 publications. EMS We’re proud of our robust emergency medical services program that includes nine state-of-the-art Geisinger Life Flight® helicopters stationed at six bases and two Critical Care Ground Transports. Education At Geisinger, ‘learning’ is one of our core values. Fortunately, our environment is uniquely suited, with diverse pathologies, patient populations, and acuities, to educate the best and brightest minds in medicine, including Geisinger Commonwealth School of Medicine students and Geisinger Emergency Medicine residents. We are excited to expand our graduate medical education of EM residents with our second EM residency program based at Geisinger Wyoming Valley in Wilkes-Barre, PA (coming in 2026). Our first program, established in 1976 at Geisinger Medical Center in Danville, PA, is one of the oldest of its kind and maintains full accreditation from the ACGME Residency Review Committee. System Triage Officers When our ED patients need expedited transfers within the health system, we rely on our System Triage Officers (STOs) to help make it happen. Geisinger’s STOs are Emergency Medicine physicians who work virtually to ensure proper patient movement throughout the health system. Trauma Our trauma centers provide immediate care for patients with severe or life-threatening injuries. Geisinger Medical Center and Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center are Level 1 trauma centers, equipped to provide the highest level of trauma care to those who are critically ill or injured. To learn more about Geisinger’s extensive trauma services, click here. Emergency medicine physicians at Geisinger have significantly broadened their influence both within and beyond the emergency department by serving in other key leadership and strategic roles, including Hospital Chief Medical Officers, Utilization Management Review, Information Technology, Population Health, Employee Health, Geisinger Health Plan, and various Hospital and System Quality Committees. Meet the rest of our Emergency Medicine Team Explore our Emergency Medicine Physician opportunities Explore our Emergency Medicine Advanced Practice Provider opportunities Our Latest Expansion Projects GMC Expansion Project GWV breaks ground on transformative expansion project Gretchen Janinia Krant PA-C Geisinger’s Emergency Medicine Advanced Practice Providers play a vital role in the care of the system’s ED patients and are valued for our contribution. The nature of our work allows for flexible scheduling with few nights – which means I’m engaged at work but also at home. I am trusted to work independently and enjoy the camaraderie of working alongside a skilled multidisciplinary team. Our APPs are given opportunities to explore passions like teaching, administration, and more. We are also represented at the system level by our esteemed Advanced Practice Council, on which I’m proud to serve! If given the opportunity, I’d choose Geisinger again (and again). Gretchen Krant David Paul Rupprecht MD Geisinger provides a robust environment to explore interests and passions. During my interview process, I was informed of the opportunities within the health system that range from administrative and academics to research and innovation. Geisinger strives for quality care with gifted, efficient, and reliable staff. I am personally thankful for the opportunities Geisinger has provided and look forward to the advancement of healthcare in our region. David Rupprecht Nancy Marie Clemens I was attracted to the work Geisinger was doing to improve the quality of emergency care children receive in north central PA. They are at the cutting edge and national experts in Pediatric Readiness, and I was excited to join the team. Additionally, I love teaching both pediatric and emergency medicine residents at the bedside and in formal academic settings. Nancy Clemens Brytanie N. Marshall One of the projects I am most proud of is related to both emergency medicine and informatics. We worked for more than a year to standardize our ED provider note template and to reduce documentation burden for providers. The note is now used more than 20,000 times a month and we successfully reduced the note length by 40%. Kyle Marshall Why join Geisinger? Benefits & support Competitive compensation package & benefits including three robust retirement plans, CME, relocation, and more! To learn more about our extensive benefits that allow our employees to live well both in their professional and personal lives, click here. Regionally competitive pay, enhanced by a lower cost of living Recruitment incentives, including monthly stipend during residency or fellowship CME - $4,500/year Fully paid relocation Medical malpractice with tail coverage Academic opportunities with Geisinger Commonwealth School of Medicine. To learn more about how Geisinger is building healthcare teams of the future, click here. Better quality of life — enjoy fresh air, short commutes, and tight-knit communities in our respective regions across Pennsylvania. To learn more about living in our service area, click here. Satisfaction of working for a nationally recognized, physician-led healthcare organization committed to leading healthcare change. To learn more about Geisinger’s many accolades, click here. To learn more about why others chose Geisinger, click here. Well-being As Emergency Medicine professionals, stress, emotional fatigue, grief, and burnout are part of the complex reality we live in. At Geisinger, we believe it's crucial to prioritize your own well-being to care for others. That’s why we created the Center for Well- Being, whose mission is to foster a culture that supports our teams, both professionally and personally. These services include 1:1 well-being consultation, peer support, group well-being workshops, Cabana virtual support, and well-being navigators. Don’t miss our 2025 events EMRA Job & Fellowship Fair on 9/7/25 in Salt Lake City, UT CareerMD Virtual Career Fairs Practice Match Career Fairs Networking Event at Topgolf Salt Lake City on 9/8/25 Living in Pennsylvania Working Here Search physician jobs Top 8 Most Innovative Healthcare Systems – Becker's Hospital Review Our Geisinger communities throughout Pennsylvania Geisinger hospital sites Geisinger joint venture Geisinger strategic partnership Geisinger Health Plan coverage area Routes *a campus of Geisinger Medical Center **a campus of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center Central region: GMC Geisinger Medical Center GSACH Geisinger Shamokin Area Community Hospital* GBH Geisinger Bloomsburg Hospital GSLH Geisinger St. Luke’s Hospital North-Central region: GJSH Geisinger Jersey Shore Hospital GMCM Geisinger Medical Center Muncy Northeastern region: GCMC Geisinger Community Medical Center GSWB Geisinger South Wilkes-Barre** GWV Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center WMH Wayne Memorial Hospital Western region: GLH Geisinger Lewistown Hospital Sign up for job alerts Sign up below to receive job alerts and communications about career opportunities with Geisinger. item.title: Auszubildender zum Fluggerätmechaniker m/w/d Fachrichtung Instandhaltungstechnik
item.description:
Mit über 35 Jahren Erfahrung im Offshore-Flugbetrieb zählt HeliService zu den Erfahrensten in der Offshore Logistik Branche. In dieser Zeit hat sich HeliService zu einem international agierenden Unternehmen entwickelt. HeliService ist Weltmarktführer im Bereich Hoist Operation und bietet neben Intelligent Offshore Logistik und HEMS auch 3rd Party Maintenance Services an. Aufgrund ihrer weitreichenden Expertise und hohen Qualitätsstandards ist HeliService auch eines von sechs internationalen Excellence Service Center von Leonardo. Die Wartung und Instandhaltung von HeliService führt routinemäßige Inspektionen entsprechend der Wartungspläne durch, um die Flugzeuge in optimalem Zustand zu halten. Darüber hinaus führt sie außerplanmäßige Reparaturen durch. Ein hohes Augenmerk wird auf die lückenlose Dokumentation und Qualitätskontrollen gelegt, um die gesetzlichen Standards zu erfüllen und ein Höchstmaß an Sicherheit und Lufttüchtigkeit zu gewährleisten. Da das Unternehmen weiter wächst, suchen wir zum 01.08.2026 wieder zwei Auszubildende zum Fluggerätmechaniker m/w/d Fachrichtung Instandhaltungstechnik. Der praktische Ausbildungsteil findet in Emden statt. Der theoretische Ausbildungsteil findet in der Berufsschule in Bremen im Blockunterricht statt. Die Fahrtkosten nach Bremen und die Unterkunft dort wird von HeliService getragen. Unser Ziel ist es mit der Ausbildung unser Freigabeberechtigtes Personal der Zukunft von morgen zu gewinnen. Es bestehen folglich umfangreiche Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten im Unternehmen und es gibt eine Übernahmegarantie nach der Ausbildung. In dieser Zeit hat sich HeliService zu einem international agierenden Unternehmen entwickelt. HeliService ist Weltmarktführer im Bereich Hoist Operation und bietet neben Intelligent Offshore Logistik und HEMS auch 3rd Party Maintenance Services an. Aufgrund ihrer weitreichenden Expertise und hohen Qualitätsstandards ist HeliService auch eines von sechs internationalen Excellence Service Center von Leonardo. Die Wartung und Instandhaltung von HeliService führt routinemäßige Inspektionen entsprechend der Wartungspläne durch, um die Flugzeuge in optimalem Zustand zu halten. Darüber hinaus führt sie außerplanmäßige Reparaturen durch. Ein hohes Augenmerk wird auf die lückenlose Dokumentation und Qualitätskontrollen gelegt, um die gesetzlichen Standards zu erfüllen und ein Höchstmaß an Sicherheit und Lufttüchtigkeit zu gewährleisten. Da das Unternehmen weiter wächst, suchen wir zum 01.08.2026 wieder zwei Auszubildende zum Fluggerätmechaniker m/w/d Fachrichtung Instandhaltungstechnik. Der praktische Ausbildungsteil findet in Emden statt. Der theoretische Ausbildungsteil findet in der Berufsschule in Bremen im Blockunterricht statt. Die Fahrtkosten nach Bremen und die Unterkunft dort wird von HeliService getragen. Unser Ziel ist es mit der Ausbildung unser Freigabeberechtigtes Personal der Zukunft von morgen zu gewinnen. Es bestehen folglich umfangreiche Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten im Unternehmen und es gibt eine Übernahmegarantie nach der Ausbildung. Ihr Profil Realschulabschluss oder Abitur mit mindestens guten Noten in Mathematik, Physik und Englisch Sehr gutes technisches Verständnis Führerschein Klasse B Mindestens gute Englischkenntnisse Fähigkeit, auf engstem Raum zu arbeiten Sehr gute Hand-Augen-Koordination und handwerkliches Geschick Bereitschaft Verantwortung zu übernehmen - Absolute Zuverlässigkeit Teamplayer, der Lust hat mit internationalen Kollegen zusammenzuarbeiten Ihre Aufgaben Ausbildung, um nach Abschluss der vorgesehenen Ausbildung, planmäßige und unplanmäßige Wartungsarbeiten an Luftfahrzeuge und deren Komponenten durchzuführen Durchlauf diverser Ausbildungsstufen gemäß dem Ausbildungsrahmenplan: Grundkenntnisse in der Metallbearbeitung wie z.B. das Bohren, Kanten, Biegen, Fügen, Nieten, Feilen und die Oberflächenbehandlungen Kontinuierliche Schulung und Einweisung in die einzelnen Systeme und Komponenten der Luftfahrzeuge Flugphysik/-technik, Werkstoffkunde, Hydraulik, Elektrik/Elektronik, Triebwerkskunde, Mathematik, technisches Englisch Unterstützung der Mechaniker bei der Durchführung von Wartungsarbeiten Routinearbeiten im Hangar und am Flugzeug dem Austausch von Komponenten bei Bedarf der Bearbeitung der notwendigen Unterlagen und Arbeitskarten Unser Angebot Abwechslungsreiche Ausbildung in einem mittelständischen, stets wachsenden Unternehmen Sehr gute Zukunftsperspektiven und Übernahmemöglichkeiten Vermögenswirksame Leistungen, betriebliche Altersvorsorge Umfangreiche Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten nach der Ausbildung Arbeitgeberzuschuss zur Mittagsverpflegung, Getränke und Fitnessangebot Regelmäßige Azubitreffen sowie Azubi Aktionstage
Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt? Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung.

item.title: Disponent m/w/d
item.description: Zur Unterstützung des stetig wachsenden Flugbetriebes von HeliService und der zugehörigen Disposition suchen wir nach Flugdienstberatern (m/w/d) für unseren Standort in Emden.Zur Unterstützung des stetig wachsenden Flugbetriebes von HeliService und der zugehörigen Disposition suchen wir nach Flugdienstberatern (m/w/d) für unseren Standort in Emden. Unsere Anforderungen Kaufmännische Berufsausbildung (idealerweise im Bereich Logistik) Erste Berufserfahrung in der Disposition wünschenswert Gute MS-Office-Kenntnisse Sehr gute Deutsch und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift Gutes mathematisches Verständnis Fähigkeit mehrere Aufgaben zeitgleich, zielorientiert zu lösen Kundenorientiertes Denken und Handeln Ihre Aufgaben Wirtschaftliche Planung und Koordination der verfügbaren Luftfahrzeuge nach den Zielvorgaben des Unternehmens und deren Kunden Kommunikation und Koordination der Luftfahrzeuge Einholung und Bearbeitung aller Genehmigungen für reguläre und außerplanmäßige Einsätze des Flugbetriebes Kommunikation mit Flugplätzen, Behörden, Kunden, Lieferanten, etc. Planung und Koordination von Schulungsmaßnahmen für Kunden Erstellung der Flugvorbereitung sowie der Flugnachbereitung als Grundlage der Rechnungsstellung Pflege von Statistiken für die Bereiche Flugbetrieb und Verwaltung Unterstützung des Flugbetriebsleiters bei der Personaleinsatzplanung Unser Angebot Eigenverantwortliche Tätigkeit in einem mittelständischen, stets wachsenden Unternehmen Kurze Entscheidungswege Vermögenswirksame Leistungen, betriebliche Altersvorsorge Vergünstigte Mittagsverpflegung und Getränke, Fitnessangebot, Bikeleasing Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt? Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung.

item.title: HEMS Commander m/f/d
item.description: Applications are invited for the position of HEMS Commander with HeliService U.K. Limited. The successful candidate will join a close-knit team operating out of a state-of-the-art heliport located next to RAF Waddington, Lincoln, which is centrally located to cover the 3,500 mile² area of operations. The Lincs & Notts Air Ambulance is one of the longest serving Air Ambulance charities in the UK, established in 1994 , and provides advanced pre-hospital care to the communities it serves. Applications are invited for the position of HEMS Commander with HeliService U.K. Limited. The successful candidate will join a close-knit team operating out of a state-of-the-art heliport located next to RAF Waddington, Lincoln, which is centrally located to cover the 3,500 mile² area of operations. The Lincs & Notts Air Ambulance is one of the longest serving Air Ambulance charities in the UK, established in 1994 , and provides advanced pre-hospital care to the communities it serves. Your profile Required Experience UK CAA ATPL(H)/IR Class 1 Medical Either: 1000 hours as pilot-in-command of which 500 hours are as pilot-in-command of helicopters or 1000 hours as co-pilot in HEMS operations of which 500 hours are as pilot-in-command under supervision and 100 hours pilot-in-command of helicopters 500 hours’ operating experience in helicopters gained in a similar operational environment 20 hours of VMC at night as pilot-in-command Desirable Experience AW169 Type Rating HEMS operations NVIS operations The successful candidate must: Have the right to live and work in the UK Be able to pass an Enhanced DBS criminal record check Your tasks Preparation of aircraft and flight documentation. To follow the Operations Manual and all associated policy and procedures. Daily flight briefings to the duty HEMS team. The safe conduct of CAT flights for the purpose of HEMS day/night & under NVIS. Maintaining strict time parameters and procedures in the interest of patients. Follow SOPs issued in partnership with our charity partners. Deliver a consistently high level of service to our customer, patients and public supporters. Meeting customer's supporters, ex-patients and undertaking public liaison events whilst on duty Duties & Responsibilities include: Delivering safe and exemplary service to our customers. Displaying a professional and conscientious attitude at all times. Being the face of the company and the customer whilst performing your role. Our offer Equal time roster 28 Days annual leave entitlement Competitive salary and pay structure Private Medical Care Life Insurance Cover Company Pension Scheme Loss of License Cover

item.title: Pilot AW169 m/f/d
item.description: We are looking for experience captains, with AW169 type rating to complete our team. The company is growing further and we would like to employ additional pilots to the next possible date. If you are an ambitious team player, who is willing to fly offshore, please feel free to apply!We are looking for experience captains, with AW169 type rating to complete our team. The company is growing further and we would like to employ additional pilots to the next possible date. If you are an ambitious team player, who is willing to fly offshore, please feel free to apply! Our requirements EASA ATPL (H) with Instrument Rating (H) Ideally AW169 type rating and offshore experience At least 2000 flight hours on helicopters Twin turbine experience EASA Class 1 Medical English language proficiency level 4+ Team-player Reliability, punctuality and a high focus on safety are expected Roster 5/2 Your tasks Preparation and conduction of flights to and from offshore installations and vessels in accordance with company procedures Briefing and debriefing of crew and passengers Conduction of helicopter hoist operations to ships, platforms and wind turbines Taking responsibility for the safety of the helicopter and its occupants Following the instructions and procedures in the operations manual Ensuring compliance with the relevant requirements of the operator’s occurrence reporting schemes Ensuring compliance with all flight and duty time limitations (FTL) and rest requirements applicable to their activities Our offering An independent work environment in a growing mid-sized company Flat hierarchy Capital-forming benefits, attractive company pension schemes Lunch subsidised by the company, free drinks Company fitness facilities, company bike leasing Are you interested? We look forward to receiving your application.

item.title: B1.3 LICENSED ENGINEER
item.description: We are currently recruiting for the following positions: B1.3 LICENSED ENGINEER Location: Essex Reporting to: Line Maintenance Supervisor Click here for more information

item.title: B1.3 Licensed Engineer
item.description: Sloane Helicopters has a long and excellent reputation for providing helicopter EMS services and has been providing air ambulance support since 1999. Due to continued expansion, we now have an exciting opportunity for a B1.3 Licensed Engineer to join the Sloane Helicopters team in support of the Essex & Herts Air Ambulance, based at North Weald and Earls Colne. This is a fantastic opportunity to utilise and develop your existing skills to make a real difference in the world and to play a vital part in saving lives. Reporting to the Line Maintenance Supervisor, this is a unique position for an engineer who is happy to work on their own initiative with a great deal of autonomy whilst, at the same time, being part of an experienced and supportive team. The successful candidate will hold a B1.3 licence and, although not essential, ideally with type-ratings for AW169/AW109 aircraft. They will also need the personal discipline to maintain the aircraft in their care to a consistent high standard. Working at Sloane is not just a job. Becoming part of the Sloane team will give you a real sense of belonging and you will be joining at a very exciting point in the company’s continued evolution. We want to nurture and develop our employees to ensure they reach their full potential and to help drive the company forward to greater success. In addition to a competitive salary, you will also be eligible to be enrolled on the new Employee Bonus Scheme which will grow in direct correlation with the company’s success. Due to the mobile nature of this role, you will also be supplied with a company van. Other benefits will include: Contributory Pension Scheme – 5% company contribution Private Healthcare Death in Service – 3x salary Income Protection Scheme If using your skills and experience to help save lives sounds like the role that you have been waiting for, please forward your CV by email, together with details of your current salary, to Mrs Andrea Peel - apeel@sloanehelicopters.com Your CALLING Charity Needs B1.3 LICENSED ENGINEERS YOU!

item.title: AW169 AND AW109 FLOATING PILOT
item.description: Due to continued expansion, we are looking to recruit pilots ideally rated on both the AW169 and AW109, to take up a ‘floating pilot’ role for both The Children’s Air Ambulance and HEMS operations. To fulfil the ‘floating pilot role’ the successful candidate must have: UK CAA ATPL(H) with IR 2000 hours minimum total time 500 hours minimum rotary time (inc. 500 hours minimum multi-engine turbine) 500 hours minimum rotary PIC 200 hours minimum IFR 100 hours minimum Night Desirable qualifications include: Multi Pilot Type Rated on AW169 TRI/TRE qualification Previous HEMS experience Single Pilot Type Rated on AW109 (E, S or SP variants) Additionally, it is essential that all applicants meet the following requirements: Have the right to live and work in the UK Full UK/EU driving licence Class 1 medical Successful applicants will be expected to reside centrally in the UK and be willing to travel to various bases as you will be expected to locate to airfields, bases and other aviation locations as required by the Company for training and operations, in accordance with the FTL scheme. Applicants must also possess the ability to communicate clearly, show leadership within a team context and work flexibly with minimum supervision. Applications will only be accepted from candidates who meet the minimum qualification requirements. Should you be interested, please forward your CV, together with details of your current salary, to vacancies@sloanehelicopters.com Benefits: Pension Scheme Private Healthcare Death in Service Loss of Licence

item.title: HEMS PILOTS
item.description: Due to continued expansion, Sloane Helicopters is recruiting experienced Pilots for its HEMS operations. You will be required to fly as a Line Pilot on a fixed roster, primarily in the Midlands region of the UK. Rostering will be in accordance with the company Flight Time Limitations scheme. Preference will be given to applicants who have previous HEMS experience, or a type rating on the AW109. It is essential that all applicants meet the following requirements: 2000 hrs (minimum of 1500 rotary hrs and 500 hrs in command) UK CPL (H) or ATPL (H) Instrument Rating (Helicopters) 500 hrs experience on multi-engine helicopters 200 hrs IFR Have the right to live and work in the UK Full UK/EU driving licence Must be willing to relocate to within 45 minutes drive of an allocated base It is desirable that applicants meet the following requirements: Previous HEMS experience Single Pilot Type Rated on AW109 (E, S or SP variants) Single Pilot Multi Engine Instrument Rating You must possess the ability to communicate clearly and concisely to team members whilst carrying out other tasks. You must be willing and flexible to work at other locations, if required, usually with notice but also occasionally on an ad hoc basis. Benefits: Pension Scheme Private Healthcare Death in Service Loss of Licence Should you be interested, and meet the requirements stated above, please forward your CV, together with details of your current salary, to vacancies@sloanehelicopters.com.

item.title: Chief Flying Instructor
item.description: We are looking to recruit a suitably qualified person to be responsible for overseeing the day to day operation of our busy Flying School at Sywell whilst ensuring the delivery of excellent service to our students/customers. Reporting to the Head of Training, the candidate will require: CPL (H) FI (H) with sub-part (f)(Night) R22 and R44 type ratings Whilst not essential, the following would also be desirable: FI(H) Subpart (e) R66 type rating Previous CAA Form 4 Nominated Person Candidates will be required to pass a UK CAA Form 4 interview with the company’s Licencing & Training Standards Inspector before being offered the role. Should you be interested in this position, please forward your CV with a covering letter to apeel@sloanehelicopters.com Closing date: Monday, 19 January

item.description: Operate as PIC while providing a professional tour for a fast and dynamic operation. Responsible for determining aircraft airworthiness while maintaining a busy schedule. Licenses and Certifications FAA Commercial Rotorcraft / Instrument Rotorcraft Technical Skills Basic computer skills required Requirements and Experience Must meet Commercial Part 135 and TOPS minimums: 1000 hours PIC Helicopter; 100 hours cross-country; 25 hours night cross-country. No previous turbine time is required. Applicants will be required to work up to a 14-hour duty day on a 7 & 7 or 4 & 3 days schedule. Applicants must be detail oriented and have excellent communication and customer service skills. item.title: Ground Operations Officer
item.description: The main purpose of the Ground Operations Officer is to help and assist the pilots and act as the link between NHV Denmark and the client. Main Responsibilities Make all the preparation for flights Responsible for planning and coordination for all NHV Denmark’s flight operations Understand the client´s business and needs, under the decision and supervision of the Ground Operations Manager Liaise with customers as required/directed on delays and significant events. Assist with customer enquires or queries as required. Liaise with line internal departments and customers as required on current operational matters. Pilots scheduling Ensure all administration is carried out to the required standard and regulations Anticipate and co-ordinate required changes to the flight schedule and liaise with relevant departments. Assist the colleagues and other ad hoc work from Operation or Ground Operations Manager Responsible for complying with Information Security Management System (ISMS) policies, procedures, and controls and supporting information security objectives Skills and Experience Skils Service minded Language: Good knowledge of Danish and English is mandatory Competent in the use of IT is mandatory Good at communications Ability to work under pressure Experience Experience in aviation environment is preferred Experience in operations or coordination is preferred Is a team player and helps out colleagues where needed Competent in the use of IT is mandatory Language: Good knowledge of Danish and English is mandatory

item.title: AW139 Co-Pilot
item.description: NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. Are you an experienced AW139 Co-Pilot ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential. Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the Den Helder base. Your Role As an AW139 Co-Pilot, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. What You’ll Be Doing Flight Preparation & Execution Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements Assisting the pilot-in-command with administrative flight duties Understanding the client’s business & needs, under the decision and supervision of the Chief Pilot Compliance & Professional Standards Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures Respect company policies including Code of Conduct, Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies Support the pilot-in-command in maintaining a proper standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance Offshore & Team Responsibilities Act responsible to the pilot-in-command in assisting to conduct an efficient flight Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot Remain flexible for operational needs Lead by example and promote team spirit Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs What You Bring Qualifications EASA CPL(H) License - mandatory UK CPL(H) License - desirable EASA Class 1 Medical - mandatory UK CAA Class 1 Medical - desirable Multi-Engine Instrument Rating - mandatory AW139 Type Rating English Proficiency Level 4 Total expected flight hours: Min. 500 hours total flight time on helicopters Min. 500 hours Multi-engine helicopter Competencies Strong ICT skills (aviation software & administrative systems) Dutch language skills (preferred but not mandatory) Safety-first mindset Professional, customer-oriented attitude Flexible and operationally resilient (e.g. for double crewing, standby storm duties ...) Team player Living within commuting distance of Den Helder Join the Thrilling World of Aviation Become part of an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful mindset with over 25 years of industry expertise. We operate in a highly professional offshore environment where teamwork, safety and technical precision are paramount. What We Offer Permanent (indefinite) contract Competitive salary package 14 days ON / 14 days OFF roster 183 duties per year Early/Late shift pattern A professional and experienced offshore aviation team 📲If you are an AW139-rated co-pilot who thrives in structured offshore operations and takes pride in delivering safe, precise and customer-focused flights — and you are locally based near Den Helder — we would love to hear from you!

item.title: Technical Director
item.description: NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. We are seeking an experienced and strategic Technical Director to lead and oversee all technical helicopter operations across our multi-base organization. Reporting directly to the CEO and serving on the Extended Executive Committee, you will hold full accountability for Maintenance (Part-145), CAMO, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, and Part-147 training. You will ensure safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations while driving performance, cost control, and fleet availability. This is a high-impact executive role combining operational leadership with strong business acumen and full P&L responsibility. You will lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, and sustainability programs, positioning the organization for future growth. This function can be performed from any of the NHV bases. Regular travel to other bases is required. Responsibilities and areas Operational & Strategic Leadership Lead and optimize all technical functions (Part-145 Maintenance, CAMO, Engineering, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, Part-147) with a strong business-driven and performance-focused approach. Develop and execute technical strategies, structures, and processes aligned with corporate objectives, ensuring efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and operational excellence. Drive organizational optimization and continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and supply chain operations. Ensure technical capabilities fully support operational delivery, customer commitments, and strategic growth objectives. Establish, monitor, and report on KPIs covering safety, compliance, service quality, cost efficiency, and fleet availability. Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and execute additional executive-level technical assignments as required. Financial & Performance Management Hold full P&L responsibility for technical operations, including maintenance budgets, supply chain expenditures, and lifecycle cost management. Drive sustainable cost optimization and total cost of ownership improvements across the fleet. Partner with Finance to ensure disciplined budgeting, forecasting, financial oversight, and structured performance reporting. Balance profitability objectives with uncompromising safety and regulatory compliance standards. People Leadership & Culture Provide strategic leadership to multi-location technical teams, building strong leadership capability, succession planning, and skills development. Establish clear performance goals, accountability frameworks, and KPI-driven management structures. Foster a culture aligned with company values, embedding safety, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Promote strong cross-functional alignment and transparent executive communication. Safety, Compliance & Governance Ensure full compliance with AOC, (B)CAA, EASA/CAA/ICAO regulations, and internal standards across all technical domains. Oversee governance of Accountable Managers and Postholders (Part-145, CAMO, Supply Chain, Fleet Management, Part-147). Maintain robust operational controls, reporting systems, documentation, approvals, and regulatory records. Champion airworthiness, safety, quality assurance, and consistent adherence to procedures and best practices. Transformation, Innovation & Continuous Improvement Lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, sustainability programs, and SAF adoption. Introduce innovative technologies and structured change management to enhance reliability, safety, availability, and cost efficiency. Drive continuous performance improvement across all technical and logistical functions. Stakeholder Management Build and maintain strong relationships with OEMs, suppliers, MROs, regulators, aviation authorities, and customers. Represent the technical organization at executive and regulatory levels. Ensure external partnerships and contractual commitments align with internal technical strategy and support business growth. Skills & Experience Extensive senior leadership experience in aviation technical management or a comparable highly regulated industry. Proven experience as (Nominated) Postholder under an AOC and/or as Accountable Manager. In-depth knowledge of aviation maintenance regulations and regulatory frameworks. Strong financial acumen, including P&L ownership and cost control. Demonstrated success in leading transformation, restructuring, and performance optimization initiatives. Experience in an international, multi-base operational environment. Solid understanding of business planning, operational management, and customer contract management. Results-driven, structured, and proactive mindset. Strong analytical capabilities with a KPI-focused approach to performance management. Professional level of English (spoken and written). What we offer Join the Thrilling World of Aviation Step into an exciting career in an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful spirit with over 25 years of industry expertise. What We Offer When you join our team, you can count on a competitive salary package. This role includes regular travel to our other offices. The home base is not fixed. If you’re a experienced and strategic leader who thrives in a hands-on, fast-paced helicopter environment and takes pride in delivering safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations, we’d love to hear from you!

item.title: Flight Operations Officer
item.description: The Flight Operations Officer is responsible for the execution of the daily base flight schedule for our base in Den Helder. They will interface with a number of internal and external stakeholders to manage the safe, compliant and efficient delivery of the program, whilst upholding a high standard of service to NHV’s clients. The position is an operational as well as administrative function, part of the operations department, and reports directly to the Service Delivery Lead. This is a part-time function (20 hours / week). Depending on the candidate and preferences, this could be extended. Responsibilities and areas Flight Planning & Coordination Prepare and maintain daily flight schedules, including flight watch and monitoring. Anticipate and coordinate required changes to the flight schedule. Provide operational paperwork and briefings to flight crews. Monitor weather and NOTAMs relevant to flight operations. Crew Management Manage pilot scheduling and ensure compliance with Flight Time Limitations (FTL). Liaise with crew members regarding operational updates and changes. Client & Stakeholder Liaison Act as a key point of contact for clients regarding flight changes, disruptions, and queries. Liaise with Air Traffic Control and airport authorities as needed. Communicate with internal departments (Engineering, Ground Operations, etc.) to ensure fleet availability and operational readiness. Administration & Compliance Ensure all operational and customer reports are completed accurately and on time. Maintain high standards of documentation in line with regulatory and company requirements. Skills & experience Strong interest in aviation operations and flight coordination; previous experience is a plus but not required. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Professional, friendly and customer-focused personality. Ability to work under pressure and adapt to changing circumstances. Proficiency in scheduling tools and operational software. Fluent in Dutch and English (spoken and written). Willingness to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays. What we offer You'll be welcomed in a young and exciting company. Based on your skills and competences, you'll be offered a competitive salary with extra legal benefits. The opportunity can be taken to widen your knowledge and skills.

What We Offer Permanent (indefinite) contract Competitive salary package 14 days ON / 14 days OFF roster 183 duties per year Early/Late shift pattern A professional and experienced offshore aviation team 📲If you are an AW139-rated pilot who thrives in structured offshore operations and takes pride in delivering safe, precise and customer-focused flights — and you are locally based near Den Helder — we would love to hear from you!

item.title: Experienced HHO Operator – Licensed Engineer
item.description: NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. Are you an experienced Helicopter Hoist Operator and licensed engineer with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights and crew change flights. The helicopter Hoist Operator plays a critical role combining technical skills as a Licensed Line Engineer working on the helicopter and performing hoist flights hoisting passengers to the wind farms. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests. Responsibilities and Main Results Areas Helicopter Hoist operator (HHO) Carry out HHO flight duties, in accordance with NHV Operations SOP's. Know the NHV procedures and the Flight Operations Manual. Inspect hoist systems, cables, hooks, harnesses, and safety equipment. Conduct safety briefings with passengers and flight crew. Coordinate passenger- and aircraft handling procedures before and after flights. Assist with the technical preparation for the flight(s). Operate hoist controls smoothly to raise/lower personnel or cargo. Provide precise directional guidance to the pilot (clearance, obstacles, positioning). Immediately halt operations if unsafe conditions arise. Use standardized aviation terminology. Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as respecting the Drugs & Alcohol policy. Line Maintenance Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable. Ensure all safety regulations are followed Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system. Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures. Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous. Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified. Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings. Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks. Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order. Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity. Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs. Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving. Skills and Experience Experience - Experience as a Helicopter Hoist Operator - Minimum 5 years of relevant experience as a licensed engineer. - Experience working in a line maintenance environment. - Engineering experience in different aviation companies is a plus. Skills - Medical class 2 is required. - Languages: English (minimum ICAO Level 4) - EASA Part 66 B1.3 license or B2 license. - Type rating H175 is preferred - ICT literate (Rusada Envision knowledge is a plus) - Good communicator: - Debriefing with the flight crew - Briefing of passengers - Handover of work from/to previous/next shift - Communication with the maintenance lead - Able to work independently when performing in a less controlled environment and able to take initiative to improve work and complete tasks within appropriate timeframe. - Problem solver / logic thinking. What we offer NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. We aim to create a leading position in the helicopter world with a primary focus on Europe and Africa, without compromising our safety. We strive to provide an increasing level of flexibility to our customers. Our services and experience present a refreshing and safe alternative in the industry. NHV cares about it’s people and invests in them to shape the best professionals in the industry. At NHV, we are dedicated to delivering excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions. Our expertise spans across Oil and Gas, Offshore Wind, Maritime Services, MRO Services, ATO Pilot Training, and P147 Technician Training. We take pride in working with a skilled and passionate team who ensure everything meets the highest industry standards. Joining NHV means becoming part of an innovative international aviation company that values motivation, enthusiasm, and dynamism. We prioritize our employees, supporting their well-being and growth with comprehensive, role-specific training. Our commitment to fostering a positive work environment includes organizing corporate events and encouraging innovative and critical thinking.

item.title: B1.3 Engineer AW139
item.description: NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. Are you an experienced EASA Part 66 B1.3 Licensed Engineer ready to work in a dynamic offshore helicopter environment? At our Den Helder base, you will play a key role in keeping our AW139 fleet mission-ready and safe. You’ll join a close-knit team of 8 engineers, working in a professional yet supportive environment where safety, teamwork and technical excellence come first. Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the base. Your Role As a B1.3 Engineer, you are responsible for performing and certifying line maintenance in accordance with approved maintenance data and company procedures. You work closely with fellow engineers under the supervision of the Shift Supervisor, reporting hierarchically to the Base Manager and functionally to the Maintenance Lead. You are more than a certifying engineer — you are a problem solver, safety ambassador and key contributor to operational continuity. What You’ll Be Doing Line Maintenance & Technical Excellence Perform and certify maintenance tasks in accordance with approved maintenance data Execute work orders while respecting all applicable restrictions Raise non-routine tasks and defect entries for unscheduled findings Ensure Independent Inspections on safety-critical tasks Record and certify all maintenance accurately in the work file Safety & Compliance Follow all safety regulations and MOE procedures Report safety hazards and occurrences via the reporting system Flag inaccurate, incomplete or unclear procedures Maintain a clean and orderly workspace with properly maintained tools Operational Support Contribute to flexible and efficient work planning Ensure maintenance is completed within operational deadlines Coordinate with Supply Chain, Stores and support services to guarantee parts availability Perform daily technical analysis and problem-solving based on crew feedback What You Bring Experience Minimum 5 years of experience as a licensed engineer At least 2 years hands-on experience on AW139 Experience in multiple aviation organizations is a plus Qualifications EASA Part 66 B1.3 License Type Rating AW139 (required) H175 or H160 Type Rating (advantageous) Skills Strong troubleshooting and logical thinking skills Confident communicator (flight crew debriefings & shift handovers) Able to work independently in less controlled environments Proactive, flexible and solution-oriented ICT literate (Rusada Envision knowledge is a plus) Professional level of English Living within commuting distance of Den Helder What We Offer Join the Thrilling World of Aviation Become part of an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful mindset with over 25 years of industry expertise. We operate in a highly professional offshore environment where teamwork, safety and technical precision are paramount. Permanent (indefinite) contract Competitive salary package 14 days ON / 14 days OFF roster 183 duties per year Early/Late shift pattern A supportive team of 8 engineers led by an experienced Shift Supervisor/Maintenance Lead 📲If you’re a certified B1.3 Engineer who thrives in a hands-on, fast-paced helicopter environment and takes pride in delivering safe, high-quality maintenance — we’d love to hear from you.
